source.title: Control gaps quick handoff notes — SOC2/ISO prep
source.content:
Purpose: quick, readable handoff for the reviewer (internal prep) — capture open control gaps, proposed short-term mitigations (POA), owners, and what to bring to next audit sync.

Context: prepping for the week-of-03-09 security & compliance review with external assessor. Focus areas: user access reviews (IA), change management (CM), vendor onboarding and attestations (VR), and incident response tabletop evidence (IR).

High-level status (notes to self):
- IA (Identity & Access): recurring reviews set up but scoping mismatch for cloud-admin role. Evidence: last review 2026-01-15 (export attached in confluence). Gap: no proof of recertification for two cloud-admins (contractor + new hire).
  - Quick POA: run ad-hoc recertify report, get sign-off from team leads. Owner: Jordan. ETA: 2026-03-01 (ask for confirmation during 1:1).
- CM (Change Management): PRs linked to release tickets for infra changes are inconsistent; some hotfixes lack post-deploy verification log. Gap severity: medium.
  - POA: add checklist item to release template and retroactively attach verification logs for last 6 hotfixes. Owner: Priya. ETA: 2026-03-06.
- VR (Vendor Risk): vendor attestation matrix shows 3rd-party monitoring vendor missing SOC2 type II report for 2025. Vendor claims compensating controls. Gap severity: high (ingestion of sensitive telemetry).
  - POA: request SOC2 report + compensating control documentation; if not provided, schedule mitigation call with vendor and legal to restrict data scope. Owner: Avery + Legal. ETA: 2026-03-10 (prep materials for external assessor).
- IR (Incident Response): tabletop ran 2025-11 but no formal after-action for last simulated incident. Evidence: notes exist but no closure ticket. Gap severity: low-medium.
  - POA: create AAR ticket, assign SRE lead to close. Owner: Ben. ETA: 2026-03-04.

Meeting agenda (30m internal prep before external):
1) 5m — quick recap of assessor scope and what's expected (remind: sampling window 2025-09 to 2026-02)
2) 10m — go through each gap above, confirm owners and immediate evidence we can surface
3) 10m — blockers (legal/vendor timelines, infra access for evidence) and mitigation proposals
4) 5m — next steps & commit for follow-ups (who emails vendor, who attaches files to confluence, timeline)

Questions to surface to assessor (prep to ask during external sync):
- For vendor attestation gaps, will a compensating control mapping (documented controls + control owner) be acceptable in lieu of a full SOC2 report if provided with signed attestations?
- Sampling expectations for access recertification: do they require proof of automated tool report or are manual sign-offs acceptable if documented?
- Change management evidence: they prefer SLA for post-deploy verification — what artifacts do they accept (runbooks, deployment logs, incident tickets)?
- IR tabletop: will a detailed AAR ticket plus evidence of remediation ownership suffice?

Artifacts to attach before meeting (place in confluence and link in chat):
- IA: latest access review CSV, list of cloud-admins, manager sign-offs (exported 2026-01-15)
- CM: sample PR with missing verification; proposed updated release checklist (draft)
- VR: vendor matrix export + email thread with vendor (ask legal to attach NDA/licensing excerpt)
- IR: tabletop notes, incident simulation timeline, proposed AAR ticket template

Follow-ups (owner + 1-line commitment):
- Jordan: run on-demand recertify and paste report link into confluence by 2026-03-01
- Priya: update release checklist and backfill verification for last 6 hotfixes by 2026-03-06
- Avery: escalate vendor SOC2 request to vendor support + Legal by 2026-03-03; prepare summary for assessor
- Ben: open AAR ticket and assign SRE by 2026-03-04; consolidate all artifacts into single confluence page after internal sync
- Legal (cc): confirm whether signed compensating attestations are adequate short-term evidence by 2026-03-05

Notes-to-self: be concise during external call — present remediation timelines, don’t over-commit. Ask assessor what would be a "show-stopper" vs "lower priority" so we can triage. Bring a single slide showing timeline & owners.

Slack snippets to send (copy/paste):
- To #sec-compliance: "Internal prep: quick sync 30m today 10:00am — will review action owners for SOC2/ISO artifacts (IA, CM, VR, IR). Please bring any missing evidence links."
- To Jordan: "Need recertify report by Mon 03/01 — ping me when posted to confluence."
- To Avery + Legal: "Vendor attestation request escalated — can you confirm if we accept signed compensating controls as interim evidence?"

Risk callouts for exec summary (one-liners):
- Vendor: potential high finding if SOC2 not produced — could require scoped exclusion or additional controls.
- Admin access recert: one-or-two missing cards could be flagged as control failure without documented recertification process.
- Change management: inconsistent artifacts increase audit sampling friction (time cost).

Quick checklist before hanging up (tickbox in my head):
- [ ] Confluence page created with all artifacts and links
- [ ] Owners pinged and commitments captured in Jira tasks
- [ ] One slide created for external assessor
- [ ] Legal looped on vendor attestation path

End of note. Keep this doc as ephemeral prep; after sync I'll copy key parts to the main confluence audit page and convert follow-ups into JIRA tickets.

source.title: Marketplace onboarding funnel — baseline metrics (AWS + GCP) — Jan–Feb 2026
source.content:
Doc purpose
- Establish baseline marketplace onboarding metrics prior to the listing refresh + onboarding hardening.
- Capture where we lose users (subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → API key → first successful request).
- Identify instrumentation gaps blocking reliable reporting.

Data sources & notes (read before using numbers)
- AWS Marketplace: partner portal lead exports + internal entitlement sync logs (partial) + HubSpot “marketplace_source=aws” deals.
- GCP Marketplace: Cloud Commerce Partner lead feed (manual export) + HubSpot “marketplace_source=gcp” + console onboarding logs (partial).
- Console steps are undercounted: we cannot currently join user sessions reliably across “subscribe” (external) and “provisioning” (our console) for all flows.
- Time window for baseline: 2025-12-15 to 2026-02-07 (8 weeks). For QoQ comparisons, see note at bottom.

Definitions
- Lead: any inbound marketplace-sourced contact/company record (HubSpot) OR partner lead email (if no HubSpot match yet).
- Subscription started: marketplace subscription initiated (where observable via partner lead data or entitlement request).
- Entitlement validated: our entitlement verification endpoint returns active entitlement for the marketplace account (or sync job marks active).
- Org provisioned/linked: user successfully creates a new Redwood org OR links subscription to an existing org.
- API key created: user creates at least one API key in console.
- First token: first successful inference response (streaming or non-streaming) within 7 days of subscription.

Executive summary (baseline)
- Overall (AWS+GCP): 412 marketplace-sourced leads → 118 subscription starts (~28.6% of leads) → 61 entitlements validated (~51.7% of subs) → 44 orgs linked/provisioned (~72.1% of validated) → 33 API keys (~75.0% of org-linked) → 24 first-token within 7 days (~72.7% of API keys; ~20.3% of subs).
- Median time-to-first-token (7-day cohort, n=24): 19h 42m.
- Largest drop-offs occur at:
  1) Subscription started → entitlement validated (entitlement propagation delay + unclear “what next” guidance)
  2) Entitlement validated → org provisioned/linked (existing-org edge cases + confusing error states)
- We do not have trustworthy “step duration” metrics between intermediate console screens due to missing event coverage and missing correlation IDs.

Funnel (counts) — overall + by marketplace

Table 1 — Funnel counts (8-week baseline)
| Segment | Leads | Subscription started | Entitlement validated | Org linked/provisioned | API key created | First token (≤7d) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Overall | 412 | 118 | 61 | 44 | 33 | 24 |
| AWS | 287 | 86 | 40 | 31 | 24 | 18 |
| GCP | 125 | 32 | 21 | 13 | 9 | 6 |

Table 2 — Step conversion rates (within segment)
| Segment | Lead→Sub | Sub→Entitlement | Entitlement→Org | Org→API key | API key→First token |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Overall | 28.6% | 51.7% | 72.1% | 75.0% | 72.7% |
| AWS | 30.0% | 46.5% | 77.5% | 77.4% | 75.0% |
| GCP | 25.6% | 65.6% | 61.9% | 69.2% | 66.7% |

Interpretation
- GCP shows better Sub→Entitlement (likely because entitlement visibility is coming mostly from users who already reach our console with the right context; selection bias), but worse Entitlement→Org (higher share of existing-org linking + confusion on org selection + fewer SE-assisted paths).
- AWS Sub→Entitlement is the main bottleneck; the tail of propagation delays is long and creates support tickets (see SUP-4821).

Time-to-first-token (TTFT)

Table 3 — TTFT distribution (first-token within 7 days)
| Segment | N (≤7d) | Median | P75 | P90 | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Overall | 24 | 19h 42m | 2d 03h | 4d 12h | Several “weekend stalls” (procurement + entitlement delays) |
| AWS | 18 | 17h 05m | 1d 19h | 4d 02h | P90 inflated by entitlement sync delay + retry loops |
| GCP | 6 | 1d 08h | 2d 21h | 5d 06h | Often blocked by org linking and missing docs clarity |

Step timing (what we can measure today)
- Entitlement activation → org linked/provisioned (measured via entitlement sync timestamp + org event): median 2h 10m (n=44), but missing for unknown fraction due to sync timestamp gaps.
- API key created → first token: median 26m (n=24) for customers who get that far (indicates docs + quickstart can materially affect TTFT).

Common drop-off points + observed reasons

1) Subscription started → entitlement validated
- Symptoms:
  - User reaches console “Validate entitlement” and gets “No active subscription found” or “Still syncing, try again later.”
  - Multiple retries; some users abandon and open support tickets.
- Primary reasons (from support notes + SE feedback):
  - Entitlement propagation delays from marketplace to Redwood sync job (tail > 2 hours).
  - Confusion about which AWS account / GCP billing account was used to subscribe.
  - Users expect the marketplace subscription to automatically create a Redwood org and an API key.
- Supporting references: Jira SUP-4821; Slack support escalation thread 1769640055.

2) Entitlement validated → org provisioned/linked
- Symptoms:
  - User has an existing Redwood org but can’t find the right org selector / linking UI.
  - “Org already linked to a different subscription” edge cases.
- Primary reasons:
  - Multi-subscription and existing-org linking flows are not well-explained.
  - Error messages are generic; users don’t know whether to wait, retry, or contact support.
- Supporting references: Linear ENG-22115; product Slack thread 1769317788.

3) Org provisioned/linked → API key created
- Symptoms:
  - Users stop after provisioning and don’t proceed to key creation.
- Primary reasons:
  - Lack of a clear “next step” CTA that matches the marketplace mental model.
  - Some enterprise buyers wait for internal approvals before generating keys.

4) API key created → first token
- Symptoms:
  - Key exists but no successful request in 7 days.
- Primary reasons:
  - SDK setup friction; unclear which base URL/model to use; confusion between “Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private” docs.
  - Missing quickstart tailored to marketplace (metering + quotas + where usage appears).
- Supporting references: GitHub redwood-examples/pr-488 (in progress).

Instrumentation gaps (blocking reliable reporting)

Gap A — No stable “external subscription id → Redwood org/user” join key in analytics
- Impact: can’t attribute console conversion and TTFT to AWS vs GCP (or SKU) reliably; current split relies on manual tags + partial logs.
- Fix: emit consistent marketplace identifiers into:
  1) entitlement verification responses (server-side)
  2) console onboarding events (client-side)
  3) first-request events (server-side)
- Owner(s): Nadia Rahman + Cole Summers
- Related: Linear ENG-22114, ENG-22111

Gap B — Missing event coverage for key onboarding steps
- Missing/spotty events:
  - “Marketplace onboarding entrypoint viewed”
  - “Entitlement validation attempted” with error code taxonomy
  - “Org linking attempted/succeeded/failed” with failure reason
  - “API key created from onboarding” vs created elsewhere
- Owner(s): Chloe Martin + Nadia Rahman
- Related: Linear ENG-22112, ENG-22114; GitHub redwood/pr-19877 (WIP)

Gap C — Step duration is not measurable end-to-end
- Issue: timestamps exist in different systems (partner portals, entitlement sync job, console events) without correlation IDs.
- Recommendation: generate an onboarding_session_id at first console entry; carry into server calls; store in billing/usage pipeline for first-token attribution.

Gap D — HubSpot source tracking is inconsistent
- Issue: marketplace deals sometimes created without “marketplace_source” populated, especially if initiated by an AE after a partner lead email.
- Fix: update RevOps process + required fields; add automation to backfill when partner lead email domain matches known marketplace patterns.
- Owner: Isabelle Laurent
- Related: Drive doc revops-process-marketplace-deal-ops.json

Baseline-quality notes / confidence rating
- Counts: medium confidence for leads + subscriptions; low-to-medium confidence for entitlement validated; medium confidence for org + API keys; medium confidence for first token within 7d (we can query usage logs but attribution to marketplace is incomplete).
- TTFT: medium confidence for the ≤7d cohort who can be attributed; likely biased toward “successful” users (missing attribution for abandoned flows).

Action items (next 2 weeks)
1) Finalize event taxonomy + error codes for onboarding
- Owner: Nadia Rahman
- Partner: Chloe Martin
- Output: event spec + dashboard plan

2) Entitlement sync job: add retry + idempotency + alert thresholds
- Owner: Ethan Park
- Output: reduce tail latency + fewer “no entitlement found” false negatives

3) Update marketplace onboarding docs to reflect real flow and edge cases
- Owner: Alex Martinez (Hosted), Kira Thompson (Dedicated/Private)
- Output: “What happens after you subscribe” + troubleshooting guide

4) RevOps: enforce marketplace fields in HubSpot workflows
- Owner: Isabelle Laurent

Appendix — Previous period quick check (for sanity)
- Prior 8 weeks (2025-10-20 to 2025-12-14): 365 leads, 97 subs, 17 first-token within 7 days.
- Current baseline shows higher first-token volume (24) but unclear if due to seasonality, more SE assistance, or true product improvement. Need better instrumentation before claiming lift.

Open questions
- Do we want TTFT SLA targets per marketplace segment (e.g., median < 6 hours)? If yes, which team owns it (Growth vs Platform vs DevEx)?
- Should “trial credits” be represented as a marketplace dimension, or should we keep it off-marketplace and drive to PLG direct sign-up?
- For Dedicated/Private marketplace procurement, should first-success metric be “first token” or “first successful provisioning + health check” (since inference may be gated by network/VPC setup)?

  - User reaches console “Validate entitlement” and gets “No active subscription found” or “Still syncing, try again later.”
  - Multiple retries; some users abandon and open support tickets.
- Primary reasons (from support notes + SE feedback):
  - Entitlement propagation delays from marketplace to Redwood sync job (tail > 2 hours).
  - Confusion about which AWS account / GCP billing account was used to subscribe.
  - Users expect the marketplace subscription to automatically create a Redwood org and an API key.
- Supporting references: Jira SUP-4821; Slack support escalation thread 1769640055.

2) Entitlement validated → org provisioned/linked
- Symptoms:
  - User has an existing Redwood org but can’t find the right org selector / linking UI.
  - “Org already linked to a different subscription” edge cases.
- Primary reasons:
  - Multi-subscription and existing-org linking flows are not well-explained.
  - Error messages are generic; users don’t know whether to wait, retry, or contact support.
- Supporting references: Linear ENG-22115; product Slack thread 1769317788.

3) Org provisioned/linked → API key created
- Symptoms:
  - Users stop after provisioning and don’t proceed to key creation.
- Primary reasons:
  - Lack of a clear “next step” CTA that matches the marketplace mental model.
  - Some enterprise buyers wait for internal approvals before generating keys.

4) API key created → first token
- Symptoms:
  - Key exists but no successful request in 7 days.
- Primary reasons:
  - SDK setup friction; unclear which base URL/model to use; confusion between “Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private” docs.
  - Missing quickstart tailored to marketplace (metering + quotas + where usage appears).
- Supporting references: GitHub redwood-examples/pr-488 (in progress).

Instrumentation gaps (blocking reliable reporting)

Gap A — No stable “external subscription id → Redwood org/user” join key in analytics
- Impact: can’t attribute console conversion and TTFT to AWS vs GCP (or SKU) reliably; current split relies on manual tags + partial logs.
- Fix: emit consistent marketplace identifiers into:
  1) entitlement verification responses (server-side)
  2) console onboarding events (client-side)
  3) first-request events (server-side)
- Owner(s): Nadia Rahman + Cole Summers
- Related: Linear ENG-22114, ENG-22111

Gap B — Missing event coverage for key onboarding steps
- Missing/spotty events:
  - “Marketplace onboarding entrypoint viewed”
  - “Entitlement validation attempted” with error code taxonomy
  - “Org linking attempted/succeeded/failed” with failure reason
  - “API key created from onboarding” vs created elsewhere
- Owner(s): Chloe Martin + Nadia Rahman
- Related: Linear ENG-22112, ENG-22114; GitHub redwood/pr-19877 (WIP)

Gap C — Step duration is not measurable end-to-end
- Issue: timestamps exist in different systems (partner portals, entitlement sync job, console events) without correlation IDs.
- Recommendation: generate an onboarding_session_id at first console entry; carry into server calls; store in billing/usage pipeline for first-token attribution.

Gap D — HubSpot source tracking is inconsistent
- Issue: marketplace deals sometimes created without “marketplace_source” populated, especially if initiated by an AE after a partner lead email.
- Fix: update RevOps process + required fields; add automation to backfill when partner lead email domain matches known marketplace patterns.
- Owner: Isabelle Laurent
- Related: Drive doc revops-process-marketplace-deal-ops.json

Baseline-quality notes / confidence rating
- Counts: medium confidence for leads + subscriptions; low-to-medium confidence for entitlement validated; medium confidence for org + API keys; medium confidence for first token within 7d (we can query usage logs but attribution to marketplace is incomplete).
- TTFT: medium confidence for the ≤7d cohort who can be attributed; likely biased toward “successful” users (missing attribution for abandoned flows).

Action items (next 2 weeks)
1) Finalize event taxonomy + error codes for onboarding
- Owner: Nadia Rahman
- Partner: Chloe Martin
- Output: event spec + dashboard plan

2) Entitlement sync job: add retry + idempotency + alert thresholds
- Owner: Ethan Park
- Output: reduce tail latency + fewer “no entitlement found” false negatives

3) Update marketplace onboarding docs to reflect real flow and edge cases
- Owner: Alex Martinez (Hosted), Kira Thompson (Dedicated/Private)
- Output: “What happens after you subscribe” + troubleshooting guide

4) RevOps: enforce marketplace fields in HubSpot workflows
- Owner: Isabelle Laurent

Appendix — Previous period quick check (for sanity)
- Prior 8 weeks (2025-10-20 to 2025-12-14): 365 leads, 97 subs, 17 first-token within 7 days.
- Current baseline shows higher first-token volume (24) but unclear if due to seasonality, more SE assistance, or true product improvement. Need better instrumentation before claiming lift.

Open questions
- Do we want TTFT SLA targets per marketplace segment (e.g., median < 6 hours)? If yes, which team owns it (Growth vs Platform vs DevEx)?
- Should “trial credits” be represented as a marketplace dimension, or should we keep it off-marketplace and drive to PLG direct sign-up?
- For Dedicated/Private marketplace procurement, should first-success metric be “first token” or “first successful provisioning + health check” (since inference may be gated by network/VPC setup)?
text:
Doc purpose
- Establish baseline marketplace onboarding metrics prior to the listing refresh + onboarding hardening.
- Capture where we lose users (subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → API key → first successful request).
- Identify instrumentation gaps blocking reliable reporting.

Data sources & notes (read before using numbers)
- AWS Marketplace: partner portal lead exports + internal entitlement sync logs (partial) + HubSpot “marketplace_source=aws” deals.
- GCP Marketplace: Cloud Commerce Partner lead feed (manual export) + HubSpot “marketplace_source=gcp” + console onboarding logs (partial).
- Console steps are undercounted: we cannot currently join user sessions reliably across “subscribe” (external) and “provisioning” (our console) for all flows.
- Time window for baseline: 2025-12-15 to 2026-02-07 (8 weeks). For QoQ comparisons, see note at bottom.

Definitions
- Lead: any inbound marketplace-sourced contact/company record (HubSpot) OR partner lead email (if no HubSpot match yet).
- Subscription started: marketplace subscription initiated (where observable via partner lead data or entitlement request).
- Entitlement validated: our entitlement verification endpoint returns active entitlement for the marketplace account (or sync job marks active).
- Org provisioned/linked: user successfully creates a new Redwood org OR links subscription to an existing org.
- API key created: user creates at least one API key in console.
- First token: first successful inference response (streaming or non-streaming) within 7 days of subscription.

Executive summary (baseline)
- Overall (AWS+GCP): 412 marketplace-sourced leads → 118 subscription starts (~28.6% of leads) → 61 entitlements validated (~51.7% of subs) → 44 orgs linked/provisioned (~72.1% of validated) → 33 API keys (~75.0% of org-linked) → 24 first-token within 7 days (~72.7% of API keys; ~20.3% of subs).
- Median time-to-first-token (7-day cohort, n=24): 19h 42m.
- Largest drop-offs occur at:
  1) Subscription started → entitlement validated (entitlement propagation delay + unclear “what next” guidance)
  2) Entitlement validated → org provisioned/linked (existing-org edge cases + confusing error states)
- We do not have trustworthy “step duration” metrics between intermediate console screens due to missing event coverage and missing correlation IDs.

Funnel (counts) — overall + by marketplace

Table 1 — Funnel counts (8-week baseline)
| Segment | Leads | Subscription started | Entitlement validated | Org linked/provisioned | API key created | First token (≤7d) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Overall | 412 | 118 | 61 | 44 | 33 | 24 |
| AWS | 287 | 86 | 40 | 31 | 24 | 18 |
| GCP | 125 | 32 | 21 | 13 | 9 | 6 |

Table 2 — Step conversion rates (within segment)
| Segment | Lead→Sub | Sub→Entitlement | Entitlement→Org | Org→API key | API key→First token |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|
| Overall | 28.6% | 51.7% | 72.1% | 75.0% | 72.7% |
| AWS | 30.0% | 46.5% | 77.5% | 77.4% | 75.0% |
| GCP | 25.6% | 65.6% | 61.9% | 69.2% | 66.7% |

Interpretation
- GCP shows better Sub→Entitlement (likely because entitlement visibility is coming mostly from users who already reach our console with the right context; selection bias), but worse Entitlement→Org (higher share of existing-org linking + confusion on org selection + fewer SE-assisted paths).
- AWS Sub→Entitlement is the main bottleneck; the tail of propagation delays is long and creates support tickets (see SUP-4821).

Time-to-first-token (TTFT)

Table 3 — TTFT distribution (first-token within 7 days)
| Segment | N (≤7d) | Median | P75 | P90 | Notes |
|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| Overall | 24 | 19h 42m | 2d 03h | 4d 12h | Several “weekend stalls” (procurement + entitlement delays) |
| AWS | 18 | 17h 05m | 1d 19h | 4d 02h | P90 inflated by entitlement sync delay + retry loops |
| GCP | 6 | 1d 08h | 2d 21h | 5d 06h | Often blocked by org linking and missing docs clarity |

Step timing (what we can measure today)
- Entitlement activation → org linked/provisioned (measured via entitlement sync timestamp + org event): median 2h 10m (n=44), but missing for unknown fraction due to sync timestamp gaps.
- API key created → first token: median 26m (n=24) for customers who get that far (indicates docs + quickstart can materially affect TTFT).

Common drop-off points + observed reasons

1) Subscription started → entitlement validated
- Symptoms:
  - User reaches console “Validate entitlement” and gets “No active subscription found” or “Still syncing, try again later.”
  - Multiple retries; some users abandon and open support tickets.
- Primary reasons (from support notes + SE feedback):
  - Entitlement propagation delays from marketplace to Redwood sync job (tail > 2 hours).
  - Confusion about which AWS account / GCP billing account was used to subscribe.
  - Users expect the marketplace subscription to automatically create a Redwood org and an API key.
- Supporting references: Jira SUP-4821; Slack support escalation thread 1769640055.

2) Entitlement validated → org provisioned/linked
- Symptoms:
  - User has an existing Redwood org but can’t find the right org selector / linking UI.
  - “Org already linked to a different subscription” edge cases.
- Primary reasons:
  - Multi-subscription and existing-org linking flows are not well-explained.
  - Error messages are generic; users don’t know whether to wait, retry, or contact support.
- Supporting references: Linear ENG-22115; product Slack thread 1769317788.

3) Org provisioned/linked → API key created
- Symptoms:
  - Users stop after provisioning and don’t proceed to key creation.
- Primary reasons:
  - Lack of a clear “next step” CTA that matches the marketplace mental model.
  - Some enterprise buyers wait for internal approvals before generating keys.

4) API key created → first token
- Symptoms:
  - Key exists but no successful request in 7 days.
- Primary reasons:
  - SDK setup friction; unclear which base URL/model to use; confusion between “Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private” docs.
  - Missing quickstart tailored to marketplace (metering + quotas + where usage appears).
- Supporting references: GitHub redwood-examples/pr-488 (in progress).

Instrumentation gaps (blocking reliable reporting)

Gap A — No stable “external subscription id → Redwood org/user” join key in analytics
- Impact: can’t attribute console conversion and TTFT to AWS vs GCP (or SKU) reliably; current split relies on manual tags + partial logs.
- Fix: emit consistent marketplace identifiers into:
  1) entitlement verification responses (server-side)
  2) console onboarding events (client-side)
  3) first-request events (server-side)
- Owner(s): Nadia Rahman + Cole Summers
- Related: Linear ENG-22114, ENG-22111

Gap B — Missing event coverage for key onboarding steps
- Missing/spotty events:
  - “Marketplace onboarding entrypoint viewed”
  - “Entitlement validation attempted” with error code taxonomy
  - “Org linking attempted/succeeded/failed” with failure reason
  - “API key created from onboarding” vs created elsewhere
- Owner(s): Chloe Martin + Nadia Rahman
- Related: Linear ENG-22112, ENG-22114; GitHub redwood/pr-19877 (WIP)

Gap C — Step duration is not measurable end-to-end
- Issue: timestamps exist in different systems (partner portals, entitlement sync job, console events) without correlation IDs.
- Recommendation: generate an onboarding_session_id at first console entry; carry into server calls; store in billing/usage pipeline for first-token attribution.

Gap D — HubSpot source tracking is inconsistent
- Issue: marketplace deals sometimes created without “marketplace_source” populated, especially if initiated by an AE after a partner lead email.
- Fix: update RevOps process + required fields; add automation to backfill when partner lead email domain matches known marketplace patterns.
- Owner: Isabelle Laurent

- TTFT: medium confidence for the ≤7d cohort who can be attributed; likely biased toward “successful” users (missing attribution for abandoned flows).

Action items (next 2 weeks)
1) Finalize event taxonomy + error codes for onboarding
- Owner: Nadia Rahman
- Partner: Chloe Martin
- Output: event spec + dashboard plan

2) Entitlement sync job: add retry + idempotency + alert thresholds
- Owner: Ethan Park
- Output: reduce tail latency + fewer “no entitlement found” false negatives

3) Update marketplace onboarding docs to reflect real flow and edge cases
- Owner: Alex Martinez (Hosted), Kira Thompson (Dedicated/Private)
- Output: “What happens after you subscribe” + troubleshooting guide

4) RevOps: enforce marketplace fields in HubSpot workflows
- Owner: Isabelle Laurent

Appendix — Previous period quick check (for sanity)
- Prior 8 weeks (2025-10-20 to 2025-12-14): 365 leads, 97 subs, 17 first-token within 7 days.
- Current baseline shows higher first-token volume (24) but unclear if due to seasonality, more SE assistance, or true product improvement. Need better instrumentation before claiming lift.

- Should “trial credits” be represented as a marketplace dimension, or should we keep it off-marketplace and drive to PLG direct sign-up?

source.title: Lifecycle Growth Sprint — Cross-functional Office Hours Notes (GTM + Product + Eng + Sales)
source.body:
Context
- Weekly cross-functional office hours during the 4-week Lifecycle Growth Experiments Sprint (Hosted API self-serve activation + early retention).
- Intended use: unblock owners quickly, align on decisions/guardrails, and capture action items.
- Primary KPIs in play: activation (first successful request + first 100k tokens within 7d), TTFT/time-to-value, D7/D30 active usage. Secondary: upgrade intent / sales-assisted handoff signals.

Standing attendees (rotates)
- Growth/Marketing: Ben Carter (owner), Tyler Benson (ops/attribution)
- Product: Marissa Cole (console), Paula Mendes (telemetry/rollouts)
- Eng: Dylan Brooks (console/tooling), Logan Wright / Nadia Rahman (telemetry/eventing), Allison Grant / Sergio Costa (dashboards)
- Sales: Jordan Blake / Stephanie Nguyen (exclusions + handoff)
- Support/QA as needed: Leila Farouk

========================
Office Hours #1 — 2026-01-14 (Kickoff week)
Attendees: Ben C, Marissa C, Dylan B, Paula M, Tyler B, Allison G

Agenda
1) Confirm activation funnel event definitions + required properties
2) Email onboarding v2: triggers/suppression + ramp plan
3) Console nudge framework: placement + “completion” definition

Notes / discussion
- Event taxonomy: we need dedupe rules to avoid inflated activation rate. Nadia flagged we have duplicate signup emissions in some flows.
- “First successful request” definition: should be any 2xx from /chat/completions OR /completions OR /responses? Current Hosted API supports chat + completions; decision needed to avoid missing older SDK calls.
- TTFT: engineering asked whether we can capture TTFT consistently across streaming + non-streaming. Paula: for now, capture server-side “first_token_ms” when available; otherwise log “response_start_ms”.
- Console nudges: debate whether to show “Create API key” nudge on landing page vs API keys page. Marissa wants nudge visible on home/dashboard, but not blocking.
- Email: ensure compliance link + unsubscribe preferences exist; there’s a plan to add console surface for preferences later (ENG-10518). For week 1, we will use standard footer unsubscribe.

Decisions
- Activation funnel stages (v1):
  1) signup_completed
  2) api_key_created
  3) first_successful_request
  4) first_100k_tokens
- first_successful_request includes: /chat/completions + /completions + SDK equivalents; exclude auth failures and 4xx/5xx.
- Console nudge “completion” definition per step:
  - API key nudge completion = api_key_created event
  - First request nudge completion = first_successful_request event
  - Usage dashboard nudge completion = usage_dashboard_viewed (new event)

Blockers / risks
- Duplicate events risk to measurement integrity (esp. signup_completed + api_key_created).
- Email suppression rules not fully documented for “already activated” cohort vs “stalled” cohort.

Action items
- Dylan / Nadia: draft dedupe approach + required props list (user_id, workspace_id, signup_ts, cohort_id, region) and share in ENG-10492 thread. Due 1/16.
- Tyler: confirm we can tie email sends to cohort_id and suppression segments. Due 1/17.
- Marissa: propose initial placements for nudge component (home + API keys page) and which are dismissible. Due 1/17.
- Allison: outline first-pass activation dashboard panels + breakdowns (region, SDK, cohort_id). Due 1/18.

========================
Office Hours #2 — 2026-01-21 (Week 1 readout prep)
Attendees: Ben C, Tyler B, Marissa C, Dylan B, Jordan B, Allison G, Sergio C

Agenda
1) Early onboarding email A/B results sanity check
2) Console nudge experiment readiness (targeting + holdout)
3) Sales exclusion rules for retargeting + lifecycle comms

Notes / discussion
- Email A/B: results show higher opens for “Latency you can trust” subject line, but click-to-activate delta unclear.
  - Concern: open tracking degraded for some privacy mail clients. Tyler suggests prioritize click + downstream activation.
- Funnel discrepancy: activation rate jumped ~+6% in EU region only. Sergio suspects event drop in another region causing denominator mismatch.
- Console nudges: feature flag cohort assignment discussed.
  - Dylan: implement stable hash by user_id + experiment_key; add explicit holdout.
  - Marissa: wants ability to target “signup-no-api-key” vs “api-key-no-request”.
- Sales: Jordan wants hard exclusion list for active opps + known enterprise domains before retargeting goes live.
  - Also request: do not send aggressive “upgrade” copy to contacts already in active evaluation with Sales.

Decisions
- Email A/B decision criterion: choose winner based on activation lift within 7 days (not open rate), with click as supporting metric.
- Console nudge targeting cohorts (v1):
  - Cohort A: Signed up, no API key within 30 mins
  - Cohort B: API key created, no first request within 2 hrs
  - Cohort C: First request done, no return session within 3 days (retention nudge)
- Sales exclusions:
  - Suppress from retargeting: contacts/domains with active opp stage >= “Discovery” (HubSpot) + list of sensitive accounts provided weekly.
  - Suppress from lifecycle “upgrade” messaging: same as above.

Blockers / risks
- Region tracking discrepancy: if not resolved, we may pause interpretation of activation changes until event integrity confirmed.
- Need a process owner for weekly exclusion list refresh.

Action items
- Sergio: open investigation ticket for region discrepancy + quick query to confirm missing events. Due 1/22.
- Dylan: confirm holdout implementation details + how we log cohort assignment event. Due 1/23.
- Jordan + Isabelle: define exclusion list export format + delivery cadence (weekly Mon 9am PT). Due 1/24.
- Ben: update retargeting brief with exclusion guardrails + share with Camila for approval. Due 1/24.

========================
Office Hours #3 — 2026-01-28 (Week 2 nudge rollout + retargeting launch checklist)
Attendees: Ben C, Marissa C, Dylan B, Paula M, Tyler B, Jordan B, Leila F

Agenda
1) Console nudge UX edge cases + QA checklist
2) Retargeting audiences + UTM conventions
3) Deliverability / “not receiving emails” reports

Notes / discussion
- Console nudge bug report: nudge sometimes appears on API key creation modal and blocks the flow (reported by Support + internal dogfood).
  - Leila has repro steps: if user navigates from “Get started” CTA → API Keys → back button → modal persists, nudge overlays.
  - Dylan: likely z-index/route state issue + dismissal persistence bug.
- Measurement: need to ensure dismissal events don’t count as completion; completion tied to underlying product event.
- Retargeting:
  - Audiences being finalized:
    1) Site visitors (pricing/docs) 30d
    2) Signup-no-activation 14d
    3) Activated-no-return 21d
  - Jordan reiterates exclusions must be applied in platform uploads.
  - Tyler confirms UTM conventions:
    - utm_source: linkedin|google
    - utm_medium: paid-social|display
    - utm_campaign: lifecycle_growth_q1_2026
    - utm_content: audience-creative-variant
- Deliverability:
  - Support has a small spike in tickets “didn’t receive onboarding email.” Hypothesis: suppression rules too aggressive OR domain reputation throttle.
  - Ben notes we’re ramping sends; need to validate DKIM/SPF/DMARC are correct and that we’re not sending to role accounts.

Decisions
- Console nudge must never block primary flows. If a nudge overlays interactive modal, treat as Sev-2 and hotfix within 24 hrs.
- Retargeting go/no-go: only launch once exclusions are confirmed live in ad platform AND UTMs validated on landing page.
- Deliverability: prioritize investigation of suppression logic for “signup-no-api-key” segment (ensure they’re not being suppressed due to “activated” misclassification).

Blockers / risks
- If console nudge bug persists, it could harm activation more than it helps.
- Deliverability issues could confound email experiment results.

Action items
- Dylan: hotfix nudge overlay behavior + ensure API key modal has nudge suppression. Link fix to SUP-24905. Due 1/29.
- Leila: run regression QA on nudge placements (home, API keys page, quickstart link) across Chrome/Safari mobile. Due 1/30.
- Tyler: validate UTMs end-to-end in analytics + ensure cohort_id captured when user arrives from ad. Due 1/30.
- Ben + Jordan: confirm exclusions successfully applied; screenshot proof from platforms for audit. Due 1/30.
- Paula: sanity check suppression segment logic vs event definitions (after dedupe fix). Due 1/31.

========================
Office Hours #4 — 2026-02-04 (Week 3 readout + final week planning)
Attendees: Ben C, Tyler B, Marissa C, Dylan B, Allison G, Sergio C, Jordan B

Agenda
1) Dashboard validity after dedupe fix
2) Interpreting conflicting signals: activation up vs retention flat
3) “Graduate to always-on” criteria for nudges/emails

Notes / discussion
- Dedupe fix merged (PR-28521). Allison/Sergio re-ran funnel backfill; pre-fix data not comparable. Mark the chart with an annotation.
- Conflicting signals:
  - Activation: improved for “API key created → first request” step after nudge rollout.
  - Retention: D7 not moving; hypothesis: faster first value but not enough guidance to build a second use case.
- Proposal: add a “Enable streaming” nudge and/or “Example app template” CTA after first request.
- Sales: Jordan wants to ensure “activated-but-no-return” retargeting doesn’t hit active opps; also wants copy to stay dev-focused (benchmarks, quickstart) not “Talk to sales.”
- Graduation criteria discussion:
  - Nudge should be “always-on” if: CTR > baseline and completion lift without increased support tickets AND no negative impact on TTFT.
  - Email: keep variant if: net activation lift and complaint rate below threshold.

Decisions
- Data integrity: treat 2/1 as the “new baseline” for funnel metrics due to dedupe fix; continue tracking trends forward.
- Nudge graduation criteria (initial):
  - +0.8pp activation lift OR -15% time-to-first-request for target cohort, with no increase in SUP tickets related to onboarding.
  - Accessibility + non-blocking behavior confirmed.
- Retargeting next step: reallocate spend toward “signup-no-activation” (best CPA) and cap “site visitors” until landing page improves.

Blockers / risks
- Risk of over-optimizing for first request without improving second session / real retention.
- Attribution: users touch email + console + ads; hard to isolate effects. Tyler recommends holdout cohorts wherever possible.

Action items
- Sergio: add alert for sudden drop in “api_key_created → first_successful_request” conversion after experiment changes. Due 2/06.
- Marissa + Ben: draft final-week proposal: post-activation nudge (streaming + template) and whether it’s in-scope as “lightweight UI.” Due 2/06.
- Tyler: document holdout strategy summary for final readout (what had holdouts vs not) + limitations. Due 2/07.
- Jordan: send updated sensitive account exclusions list before we expand retargeting. Due 2/07.

========================
Loose ends / parking lot (carry to async)
- Need a single doc that maps event names → dashboards → experiment metrics (Paula asked; otherwise everyone re-derives logic).
- Clarify whether “first_100k_tokens” should count only generation tokens or include prompt tokens; Nadia leaning “total tokens” as metered.
- Consider adding a lightweight “What to build next” console card after first request (but check out-of-scope vs “lightweight UI component”).
- Support feedback loop: create a label for onboarding-related tickets so we can monitor if experiments increase load.

Escalation paths (for quick reference)
- Console nudge breaks onboarding flow → Dylan Brooks + Marissa Cole, tag Support ticket
- Dashboard/funnel mismatch → Sergio Costa + Allison Grant
- Deliverability issue / suppression bugs → Ben Carter + Tyler Benson (loop in external deliverability support if complaint/bounce spikes)
- Sales exclusions / sensitive accounts → Jordan Blake (+ Isabelle Laurent for CRM export)

- Allison: outline first-pass activation dashboard panels + breakdowns (region, SDK, cohort_id). Due 1/18.

========================
Office Hours #2 — 2026-01-21 (Week 1 readout prep)
Attendees: Ben C, Tyler B, Marissa C, Dylan B, Jordan B, Allison G, Sergio C

Agenda
1) Early onboarding email A/B results sanity check
2) Console nudge experiment readiness (targeting + holdout)
3) Sales exclusion rules for retargeting + lifecycle comms

Notes / discussion
- Email A/B: results show higher opens for “Latency you can trust” subject line, but click-to-activate delta unclear.
  - Concern: open tracking degraded for some privacy mail clients. Tyler suggests prioritize click + downstream activation.
- Funnel discrepancy: activation rate jumped ~+6% in EU region only. Sergio suspects event drop in another region causing denominator mismatch.
- Console nudges: feature flag cohort assignment discussed.
  - Dylan: implement stable hash by user_id + experiment_key; add explicit holdout.
  - Marissa: wants ability to target “signup-no-api-key” vs “api-key-no-request”.
- Sales: Jordan wants hard exclusion list for active opps + known enterprise domains before retargeting goes live.
  - Also request: do not send aggressive “upgrade” copy to contacts already in active evaluation with Sales.

Decisions
- Email A/B decision criterion: choose winner based on activation lift within 7 days (not open rate), with click as supporting metric.
- Console nudge targeting cohorts (v1):
  - Cohort A: Signed up, no API key within 30 mins
  - Cohort B: API key created, no first request within 2 hrs
  - Cohort C: First request done, no return session within 3 days (retention nudge)
- Sales exclusions:
  - Suppress from retargeting: contacts/domains with active opp stage >= “Discovery” (HubSpot) + list of sensitive accounts provided weekly.
  - Suppress from lifecycle “upgrade” messaging: same as above.

Blockers / risks
- Region tracking discrepancy: if not resolved, we may pause interpretation of activation changes until event integrity confirmed.
- Need a process owner for weekly exclusion list refresh.

Action items
- Sergio: open investigation ticket for region discrepancy + quick query to confirm missing events. Due 1/22.
- Dylan: confirm holdout implementation details + how we log cohort assignment event. Due 1/23.
- Jordan + Isabelle: define exclusion list export format + delivery cadence (weekly Mon 9am PT). Due 1/24.
- Ben: update retargeting brief with exclusion guardrails + share with Camila for approval. Due 1/24.

========================
Office Hours #3 — 2026-01-28 (Week 2 nudge rollout + retargeting launch checklist)
Attendees: Ben C, Marissa C, Dylan B, Paula M, Tyler B, Jordan B, Leila F

Agenda
1) Console nudge UX edge cases + QA checklist
2) Retargeting audiences + UTM conventions
3) Deliverability / “not receiving emails” reports

Notes / discussion
- Console nudge bug report: nudge sometimes appears on API key creation modal and blocks the flow (reported by Support + internal dogfood).
  - Leila has repro steps: if user navigates from “Get started” CTA → API Keys → back button → modal persists, nudge overlays.
  - Dylan: likely z-index/route state issue + dismissal persistence bug.
- Measurement: need to ensure dismissal events don’t count as completion; completion tied to underlying product event.
- Retargeting:
  - Audiences being finalized:
    1) Site visitors (pricing/docs) 30d
    2) Signup-no-activation 14d
    3) Activated-no-return 21d
  - Jordan reiterates exclusions must be applied in platform uploads.
  - Tyler confirms UTM conventions:
    - utm_source: linkedin|google
    - utm_medium: paid-social|display
    - utm_campaign: lifecycle_growth_q1_2026
    - utm_content: audience-creative-variant
- Deliverability:
  - Support has a small spike in tickets “didn’t receive onboarding email.” Hypothesis: suppression rules too aggressive OR domain reputation throttle.
  - Ben notes we’re ramping sends; need to validate DKIM/SPF/DMARC are correct and that we’re not sending to role accounts.

Decisions
- Console nudge must never block primary flows. If a nudge overlays interactive modal, treat as Sev-2 and hotfix within 24 hrs.
- Retargeting go/no-go: only launch once exclusions are confirmed live in ad platform AND UTMs validated on landing page.
- Deliverability: prioritize investigation of suppression logic for “signup-no-api-key” segment (ensure they’re not being suppressed due to “activated” misclassification).

Blockers / risks
- If console nudge bug persists, it could harm activation more than it helps.
- Deliverability issues could confound email experiment results.

Action items
- Dylan: hotfix nudge overlay behavior + ensure API key modal has nudge suppression. Link fix to SUP-24905. Due 1/29.
- Leila: run regression QA on nudge placements (home, API keys page, quickstart link) across Chrome/Safari mobile. Due 1/30.
- Tyler: validate UTMs end-to-end in analytics + ensure cohort_id captured when user arrives from ad. Due 1/30.
- Ben + Jordan: confirm exclusions successfully applied; screenshot proof from platforms for audit. Due 1/30.
- Paula: sanity check suppression segment logic vs event definitions (after dedupe fix). Due 1/31.

========================
Office Hours #4 — 2026-02-04 (Week 3 readout + final week planning)
Attendees: Ben C, Tyler B, Marissa C, Dylan B, Allison G, Sergio C, Jordan B

Agenda
1) Dashboard validity after dedupe fix
2) Interpreting conflicting signals: activation up vs retention flat
3) “Graduate to always-on” criteria for nudges/emails

Notes / discussion
- Dedupe fix merged (PR-28521). Allison/Sergio re-ran funnel backfill; pre-fix data not comparable. Mark the chart with an annotation.
- Conflicting signals:
  - Activation: improved for “API key created → first request” step after nudge rollout.
  - Retention: D7 not moving; hypothesis: faster first value but not enough guidance to build a second use case.
- Proposal: add a “Enable streaming” nudge and/or “Example app template” CTA after first request.
- Sales: Jordan wants to ensure “activated-but-no-return” retargeting doesn’t hit active opps; also wants copy to stay dev-focused (benchmarks, quickstart) not “Talk to sales.”
- Graduation criteria discussion:
  - Nudge should be “always-on” if: CTR > baseline and completion lift without increased support tickets AND no negative impact on TTFT.
  - Email: keep variant if: net activation lift and complaint rate below threshold.

Decisions
- Data integrity: treat 2/1 as the “new baseline” for funnel metrics due to dedupe fix; continue tracking trends forward.
- Nudge graduation criteria (initial):
  - +0.8pp activation lift OR -15% time-to-first-request for target cohort, with no increase in SUP tickets related to onboarding.
  - Accessibility + non-blocking behavior confirmed.
- Retargeting next step: reallocate spend toward “signup-no-activation” (best CPA) and cap “site visitors” until landing page improves.

Blockers / risks
- Risk of over-optimizing for first request without improving second session / real retention.
- Attribution: users touch email + console + ads; hard to isolate effects. Tyler recommends holdout cohorts wherever possible.

Action items
- Sergio: add alert for sudden drop in “api_key_created → first_successful_request” conversion after experiment changes. Due 2/06.
- Marissa + Ben: draft final-week proposal: post-activation nudge (streaming + template) and whether it’s in-scope as “lightweight UI.” Due 2/06.
- Tyler: document holdout strategy summary for final readout (what had holdouts vs not) + limitations. Due 2/07.
- Jordan: send updated sensitive account exclusions list before we expand retargeting. Due 2/07.

- Need a single doc that maps event names → dashboards → experiment metrics (Paula asked; otherwise everyone re-derives logic).
- Clarify whether “first_100k_tokens” should count only generation tokens or include prompt tokens; Nadia leaning “total tokens” as metered.
- Consider adding a lightweight “What to build next” console card after first request (but check out-of-scope vs “lightweight UI component”).
- Support feedback loop: create a label for onboarding-related tickets so we can monitor if experiments increase load.

Escalation paths (for quick reference)
- Console nudge breaks onboarding flow → Dylan Brooks + Marissa Cole, tag Support ticket
- Dashboard/funnel mismatch → Sergio Costa + Allison Grant
- Deliverability issue / suppression bugs → Ben Carter + Tyler Benson (loop in external deliverability support if complaint/bounce spikes)
- Sales exclusions / sensitive accounts → Jordan Blake (+ Isabelle Laurent for CRM export)
text:
Context
- Weekly cross-functional office hours during the 4-week Lifecycle Growth Experiments Sprint (Hosted API self-serve activation + early retention).
- Intended use: unblock owners quickly, align on decisions/guardrails, and capture action items.
- Primary KPIs in play: activation (first successful request + first 100k tokens within 7d), TTFT/time-to-value, D7/D30 active usage. Secondary: upgrade intent / sales-assisted handoff signals.

Standing attendees (rotates)
- Growth/Marketing: Ben Carter (owner), Tyler Benson (ops/attribution)
- Product: Marissa Cole (console), Paula Mendes (telemetry/rollouts)
- Eng: Dylan Brooks (console/tooling), Logan Wright / Nadia Rahman (telemetry/eventing), Allison Grant / Sergio Costa (dashboards)
- Sales: Jordan Blake / Stephanie Nguyen (exclusions + handoff)
- Support/QA as needed: Leila Farouk

========================
Office Hours #1 — 2026-01-14 (Kickoff week)
Attendees: Ben C, Marissa C, Dylan B, Paula M, Tyler B, Allison G

Agenda
1) Confirm activation funnel event definitions + required properties
2) Email onboarding v2: triggers/suppression + ramp plan
3) Console nudge framework: placement + “completion” definition

Notes / discussion
- Event taxonomy: we need dedupe rules to avoid inflated activation rate. Nadia flagged we have duplicate signup emissions in some flows.
- “First successful request” definition: should be any 2xx from /chat/completions OR /completions OR /responses? Current Hosted API supports chat + completions; decision needed to avoid missing older SDK calls.
- TTFT: engineering asked whether we can capture TTFT consistently across streaming + non-streaming. Paula: for now, capture server-side “first_token_ms” when available; otherwise log “response_start_ms”.
- Console nudges: debate whether to show “Create API key” nudge on landing page vs API keys page. Marissa wants nudge visible on home/dashboard, but not blocking.
- Email: ensure compliance link + unsubscribe preferences exist; there’s a plan to add console surface for preferences later (ENG-10518). For week 1, we will use standard footer unsubscribe.

Decisions
- Activation funnel stages (v1):
  1) signup_completed
  2) api_key_created
  3) first_successful_request
  4) first_100k_tokens
- first_successful_request includes: /chat/completions + /completions + SDK equivalents; exclude auth failures and 4xx/5xx.
- Console nudge “completion” definition per step:
  - API key nudge completion = api_key_created event
  - First request nudge completion = first_successful_request event
  - Usage dashboard nudge completion = usage_dashboard_viewed (new event)

Blockers / risks
- Duplicate events risk to measurement integrity (esp. signup_completed + api_key_created).
- Email suppression rules not fully documented for “already activated” cohort vs “stalled” cohort.

Action items
- Dylan / Nadia: draft dedupe approach + required props list (user_id, workspace_id, signup_ts, cohort_id, region) and share in ENG-10492 thread. Due 1/16.
- Tyler: confirm we can tie email sends to cohort_id and suppression segments. Due 1/17.
- Marissa: propose initial placements for nudge component (home + API keys page) and which are dismissible. Due 1/17.
- Allison: outline first-pass activation dashboard panels + breakdowns (region, SDK, cohort_id). Due 1/18.

source.title: Lifecycle Growth Experiments Sprint — Experiment Backlog (ICE scoring, working)
source.body:
Purpose / how to use this backlog
- Working list of ideas for the 4-week Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint (Hosted API activation + early retention).
- Prioritize by ICE. This is intentionally messy; we’ll prune weekly.
- ICE definition (rough): Impact (1-10), Confidence (1-10), Effort (1-10). ICE Score = (Impact * Confidence) / Effort.
- Notes column includes open questions + gotchas + “we should ask ___”.

Guardrails / non-goals (sticky notes)
- Do NOT target active enterprise opps w/ retargeting. Jordan/Stephanie to provide exclusion list; Tyler to implement in audiences.
- CAN-SPAM/GDPR: unsubscribe + preferences must be respected everywhere (email sends + in-console surfaces). See ENG-10518 + PR-28505.
- Measurement integrity: event taxonomy + dedupe is mandatory before we judge lifts. ENG-10492 + PR-28431 + PR-28521.
- Don’t ship “big” UX changes; keep to nudges/checklist + lightweight steps + feature flags.

Key milestones we’re optimizing for (for reference)
- Activation: % new signups reaching (a) First Successful Request (FSR) and (b) First 100k tokens within 7 days
- Time-to-first-token (TTFT) + time-to-first-value
- D7/D30 active usage (early retention)

Backlog table
Columns: ID | Area | Experiment idea | Hypothesis | Primary metric | Segment | Impact | Conf | Effort | ICE | Dependencies | Owner | Status | Notes

E-01 | Email | “Welcome / API key in 2 mins” email w/ 1 CTA + code snippet | If we reduce cognitive load in first email, more users create API key + hit FSR faster | API key created rate; FSR within 7d; TTFT | New signups (all) | 9 | 7 | 3 | 21.0 | PM-659; email template refresh DES-311; ENG-10518 | Ben Carter | in_progress | Need to align snippet w/ quickstart (redwood-quickstarts/pr-412). Subject line debate: latency vs cost vs reliability.
E-02 | Email | Branching drip: “No API key after 2h” nudge w/ console deep link | If we catch stalled users early, we recover drop-off at API key step | API key created within 24h; FSR within 7d | Signup-no-key cohort | 8 | 6 | 4 | 12.0 | Trigger logic; suppression rules; ENG-10518 | Tyler Benson | queued | Question: do we have reliable “visited console” signal to avoid spam? Paula said yes but verify.
E-03 | Email | “First request failed?” troubleshooting email (SDK error + fixes) | If we proactively unblock common errors, we increase FSR and reduce support tickets | FSR within 7d; support tickets tagged onboarding | Signup + first_request_failed | 7 | 6 | 5 | 8.4 | Event property “error_code”; SDK improvements (pr-688) | Ben Carter | queued | Needs event property parity across TS/Python + API. Potentially tie to docs PR (redwood-docs/pr-913).
E-04 | Email | Replace feature list w/ “3 things you can do today” (streaming, JSON mode, embeddings) | Concrete “next actions” increase 100k tokens milestone (beyond FSR) | 100k tokens within 7d; D7 active | First-success users | 7 | 5 | 5 | 7.0 | PM-659 | Soojin Lee | draft | Messaging guideline: avoid overpromising “SLA” in self-serve email. Keep to latency/cost/reliability proof points.
E-05 | Email | Add “Book 15 min” CTA only for high-intent signals (e.g., 10+ requests in first day) | If we route high-intent users to help earlier, we improve retention and enterprise handoff quality | D7 active; meeting booked rate; opp creation rate | High-usage new users | 6 | 4 | 6 | 4.0 | Sales handoff rules; Jordan exclusions | Ben Carter | parked | Risk: pulls users into sales too early / hurts PLG. Needs Jordan + Stephanie sign-off.

C-01 | Console nudges | Onboarding checklist (API key → first request → enable streaming → view usage dashboard) | Visible progress reduces time-to-first-value | Checklist completion; FSR; 100k tokens | New signups | 9 | 6 | 6 | 9.0 | PM-663; PR-28458; ENG-10501 flags | Marissa Cole | in_progress | Need careful dismissal behavior (bug seen in SUP-24905). Keep non-blocking.
C-02 | Console nudges | “Create API key” nudge on empty-project state | Contextual CTA at moment-of-need increases API key creation | API key created rate | Signup-no-key | 8 | 7 | 4 | 14.0 | Nudge placements; ENG-10501 | Kenji Nakamura | queued | Confirm where empty state lives for orgs w/ multiple projects. Also: do we show for invited members?
C-03 | Console nudges | “Run Hello World” copy + embedded snippet w/ language toggle (curl/TS/Python) | In-console snippet reduces doc switching and improves TTFT | TTFT; FSR | New signups | 8 | 5 | 7 | 5.7 | redwood-quickstarts/pr-412 alignment; console component work | Dylan Brooks | draft | Might be too heavy for sprint (effort?). Could start as “copy + link” then iterate.
C-04 | Console nudges | After API key created: nudge “Test request in your terminal” w/ copy-pastable curl | Immediately after key creation, users are primed to try first request | FSR within 24h of key creation | Key-created users | 7 | 6 | 4 | 10.5 | Event: api_key_created; PR-28431 | Marissa Cole | queued | Watch for copy/paste key leakage concerns; add “Keep your key secret” callout.
C-05 | Console nudges | “Enable streaming” nudge only after user runs non-streaming request | Streaming perceived latency improvement increases D7 usage | D7 active; streaming enabled rate | Activated users | 6 | 4 | 5 | 4.8 | Event: request_made{streaming:false}; PR-28431 | Paula Mendes | parked | Risk: confounds retention measure; may be too subtle in 4 weeks.
C-06 | Console nudges | “View usage dashboard” nudge after first 10k tokens | Seeing spend/usage increases trust and continued use | D7 active; dashboard viewed rate | Activated users | 5 | 5 | 3 | 8.3 | Dashboard exists; ensure link stable | Marissa Cole | draft | Might also reduce support: “where are my tokens?” questions.
C-07 | Console | Email preferences link in settings + confirmation | Reduces complaints + improves deliverability by making preferences obvious | Complaint rate; unsub issues; support tickets | All users | 5 | 8 | 3 | 13.3 | ENG-10518; PR-28505 | Ethan Park | shipped | Shipped behind flag first; verify in EU flows for GDPR wording.

R-01 | Retargeting | Signup-no-activation audience (1–14 days) with dev-first creative + “Hello World” landing | Retargeting brings back stalled users and increases FSR | FSR within 7d of click; CAC/activated | Signup-no-activation | 7 | 5 | 6 | 5.8 | PM-670; audience defs; Jordan exclusions | Tyler Benson | in_progress | Strict suppression list required (enterprise domains + active opps). UTMs finalized 1/??.
R-02 | Retargeting | Site visitors (docs/quickstart) who didn’t signup (7 days) | Capture intent users and lift signup volume at acceptable CPA | Signup conversion; CPA signup | Anonymous visitors | 5 | 6 | 5 | 6.0 | PM-670; cookie consent constraints | Tyler Benson | queued | Check w/ Olga on EU consent & lookback windows. Might be ok only NA initially.
R-03 | Retargeting | Activated-but-no-return (activated in last 30d, no activity 7d) w/ “ship your first feature” examples | Bring back users after initial test drive → improve D30 | D30 active; cost per reactivated | Activated-no-return | 6 | 4 | 6 | 4.0 | PM-670; event pipeline reliability | Ben Carter | queued | Needs clear definition of “return” and exclude users who churned intentionally.
R-04 | Retargeting | Creative A/B: “Latency you can trust” vs “Lower $/token” | Identify strongest message for early-stage activation | CTR; activation per click | Retargeting cohorts | 5 | 7 | 3 | 11.7 | Creative kit DES-327 | Soojin Lee | queued | Keep claims tight (no hard numbers unless we can back up).
R-05 | Retargeting | Landing page A/B: quickstart vs console checklist | Better landing match increases conversion from click → FSR | FSR within 7d of click | Retargeting cohorts | 6 | 4 | 7 | 3.4 | Docs changes; console readiness | Marissa Cole | parked | Might be too many moving parts; do after we stabilize checklist.

M-01 | Measurement | Fix duplicate signup event emission + backfill sanity checks | Removes noise and improves confidence in experiment results | Event dedupe rate; funnel accuracy | All | 9 | 9 | 2 | 40.5 | PR-28521; ENG-10492 | Nadia Rahman | shipped | DONE. Need to re-run baseline funnel for last 14 days and annotate dashboard.
M-02 | Measurement | Standard event taxonomy + required properties for funnel | Consistent properties enable breakdowns (region, sdk, model) | % events with required props | All | 8 | 7 | 4 | 14.0 | ENG-10492; PR-28431 | Logan Wright | in_progress | Required props list: user_id, org_id, project_id, region, sdk, request_type, model.
M-03 | Measurement | Holdout group framework for nudges + email (randomized cohorts) | True holdout reduces false positives | Valid holdout assignment rate | New signups | 8 | 6 | 5 | 9.6 | PR-28477; ENG-10501 | Jada Williams | in_progress | Ensure cohort consistent across surfaces; watch for “multiple devices” issues.
M-04 | Measurement | Activation funnel dashboard w/ alerts for sudden drops | Faster detection prevents prolonged bad experiment impacts | Time to detect; alert precision | Internal | 7 | 8 | 4 | 14.0 | ENG-10533; observability-pack/pr-241 | Allison Grant | in_progress | Define alert thresholds: e.g. -15% FSR vs 7-day avg post-launch.
M-05 | Measurement | Add breakdown by “first request outcome” (success/fail/timeout) | Pinpoints where to focus (docs vs infra vs auth) | Share of failures; time to resolve | New signups | 7 | 5 | 6 | 5.8 | ENG-10492; API instrumentation | Mei Lin | draft | Needs error taxonomy alignment w/ SDK. Potentially correlate w/ support tickets tags.

S-01 | Support / Ops | Deliverability ramp plan + domain reputation monitoring checklist | Prevent bounce/complaint spikes when scaling sends | Bounce rate; complaint rate; inbox placement proxy | Email recipients | 9 | 7 | 3 | 21.0 | Risk log; INT-3301 | Tyler Benson | in_progress | Keep complaint rate <0.08% and hard bounces <1%. If higher: pause new segments first.
S-02 | Support / Ops | “Didn’t receive onboarding email” self-serve troubleshooting page (status + tips) | Reduce support load and improve trust | Ticket volume; resolution time | All | 5 | 6 | 4 | 7.5 | SUP-24871; docs update | Ben Carter | queued | Add: check spam, allowlist domain, verify preferences, resend button? (careful)

D-01 | Docs/DevEx | Align quickstart flow with onboarding CTAs (single canonical snippet) | Consistency reduces confusion and accelerates activation | TTFT; doc bounce | New signups | 8 | 6 | 5 | 9.6 | redwood-docs/pr-913; quickstarts/pr-412 | Nikhil Sharma | in_progress | Make sure email + console use SAME model name and endpoint (no drift).
D-02 | Docs/DevEx | Minimal chat app template promoted post-FSR | Example app increases 100k tokens milestone + D7 retention | 100k tokens; D7 active | First-success users | 6 | 5 | 7 | 4.3 | redwood-examples/pr-377 | Nikhil Sharma | queued | Might be too late in funnel for sprint, but low-risk to test as “optional next step”.

Misc / parking lot (not scored yet)
- “Nudge to add payment method” after 100k tokens? (probably out of scope / pricing change adjacent) — parked
- “Invite teammate” nudge to increase org stickiness — needs product alignment
- “In-console tooltip on rate limits + retries” (developer frustration) — could help retention
- “Live chat / office hours prompt for stuck users” — support staffing question

Weekly pruning notes
- Week 1: prioritize measurement + email v2 A/B + initial nudge framework; retargeting audiences drafted but don’t launch until exclusions are solid.
- Week 2: ship checklist + key creation nudge; verify holdout and dashboard before calling winners.
- Week 3: launch retargeting (small budget) + iterate creative; monitor support and deliverability.
- Week 4: consolidate learnings; graduate 1–2 nudges to always-on if no negative signals.

Open questions (TODO)
1) What is our canonical definition of “first successful request”? Does it include streaming chunk start or only full response? (Paula/Logan)
2) Region-based tracking discrepancy: are we missing events in eu-west? (refer to INT-3310)
3) How are we handling invited users vs self-serve signups in lifecycle email triggers? (Tyler)
4) Any legal restrictions on retargeting audiences for regulated domains? (Olga + Michael)

Changelog (quick + messy)
- 2026-01-12: added measurement items M-01..M-04 after dashboard review; bumped priority.
- 2026-01-18: added console preferences link item (C-07) per compliance review.
- 2026-01-24: added support tickets S-02 after spike in “no email received” complaints.
- 2026-02-06: updated statuses post PR-28521 dedupe fix; re-scored C-01 effort up (QA complexity).

title: Lifecycle Growth Experiments Sprint — Experiment Backlog (ICE scoring, working)
preview:
Purpose / how to use this backlog
- Working list of ideas for the 4-week Lifecycle Growth Experiments sprint (Hosted API activation + early retention).
- Prioritize by ICE. This is intentionally messy; we’ll prune weekly.
- ICE definition (rough): Impact (1-10), Confidence (1-10), Effort (1-10). ICE Score = (Impact * Confidence) / Effort.
- Notes column includes open questions + gotchas + “we should ask ___”.

Guardrails / non-goals (sticky notes)
- Do NOT target active enterprise opps w/ retargeting. Jordan/Stephanie to provide exclusion list; Tyler to implement in audiences.
- CAN-SPAM/GDPR: unsubscribe + preferences must be respected everywhere (email sends + in-console surfaces). See ENG-10518 + PR-28505.
- Measurement integrity: event taxonomy + dedupe is mandatory before we judge lifts. ENG-10492 + PR-28431 + PR-28521.
- Don’t ship “big” UX changes; keep to nudges/checklist + lightweight steps + feature flags.

Key milestones we’re optimizing for (for reference)
- Activation: % new signups reaching (a) First Successful Request (FSR) and (b) First 100k tokens within 7 days
- Time-to-first-token (TTFT) + time-to-first-value
- D7/D30 active usage (early retention)

Backlog table
Columns: ID | Area | Experiment idea | Hypothesis | Primary metric | Segment | Impact | Conf | Effort | ICE | Dependencies | Owner | Status | Notes

E-01 | Email | “Welcome / API key in 2 mins” email w/ 1 CTA + code snippet | If we reduce cognitive load in first email, more users create API key + hit FSR faster | API key created rate; FSR within 7d; TTFT | New signups (all) | 9 | 7 | 3 | 21.0 | PM-659; email template refresh DES-311; ENG-10518 | Ben Carter | in_progress | Need to align snippet w/ quickstart (redwood-quickstarts/pr-412). Subject line debate: latency vs cost vs reliability.
E-02 | Email | Branching drip: “No API key after 2h” nudge w/ console deep link | If we catch stalled users early, we recover drop-off at API key step | API key created within 24h; FSR within 7d | Signup-no-key cohort | 8 | 6 | 4 | 12.0 | Trigger logic; suppression rules; ENG-10518 | Tyler Benson | queued | Question: do we have reliable “visited console” signal to avoid spam? Paula said yes but verify.
E-03 | Email | “First request failed?” troubleshooting email (SDK error + fixes) | If we proactively unblock common errors, we increase FSR and reduce support tickets | FSR within 7d; support tickets tagged onboarding | Signup + first_request_failed | 7 | 6 | 5 | 8.4 | Event property “error_code”; SDK improvements (pr-688) | Ben Carter | queued | Needs event property parity across TS/Python + API. Potentially tie to docs PR (redwood-docs/pr-913).
E-04 | Email | Replace feature list w/ “3 things you can do today” (streaming, JSON mode, embeddings) | Concrete “next actions” increase 100k tokens milestone (beyond FSR) | 100k tokens within 7d; D7 active | First-success users | 7 | 5 | 5 | 7.0 | PM-659 | Soojin Lee | draft | Messaging guideline: avoid overpromising “SLA” in self-serve email. Keep to latency/cost/reliability proof points.
E-05 | Email | Add “Book 15 min” CTA only for high-intent signals (e.g., 10+ requests in first day) | If we route high-intent users to help earlier, we improve retention and enterprise handoff quality | D7 active; meeting booked rate; opp creation rate | High-usage new users | 6 | 4 | 6 | 4.0 | Sales handoff rules; Jordan exclusions | Ben Carter | parked | Risk: pulls users into sales too early / hurts PLG. Needs Jordan + Stephanie sign-off.

C-01 | Console nudges | Onboarding checklist (API key → first request → enable streaming → view usage dashboard) | Visible progress reduces time-to-first-value | Checklist completion; FSR; 100k tokens | New signups | 9 | 6 | 6 | 9.0 | PM-663; PR-28458; ENG-10501 flags | Marissa Cole | in_progress | Need careful dismissal behavior (bug seen in SUP-24905). Keep non-blocking.
C-02 | Console nudges | “Create API key” nudge on empty-project state | Contextual CTA at moment-of-need increases API key creation | API key created rate | Signup-no-key | 8 | 7 | 4 | 14.0 | Nudge placements; ENG-10501 | Kenji Nakamura | queued | Confirm where empty state lives for orgs w/ multiple projects. Also: do we show for invited members?
C-03 | Console nudges | “Run Hello World” copy + embedded snippet w/ language toggle (curl/TS/Python) | In-console snippet reduces doc switching and improves TTFT | TTFT; FSR | New signups | 8 | 5 | 7 | 5.7 | redwood-quickstarts/pr-412 alignment; console component work | Dylan Brooks | draft | Might be too heavy for sprint (effort?). Could start as “copy + link” then iterate.
C-04 | Console nudges | After API key created: nudge “Test request in your terminal” w/ copy-pastable curl | Immediately after key creation, users are primed to try first request | FSR within 24h of key creation | Key-created users | 7 | 6 | 4 | 10.5 | Event: api_key_created; PR-28431 | Marissa Cole | queued | Watch for copy/paste key leakage concerns; add “Keep your key secret” callout.
C-05 | Console nudges | “Enable streaming” nudge only after user runs non-streaming request | Streaming perceived latency improvement increases D7 usage | D7 active; streaming enabled rate | Activated users | 6 | 4 | 5 | 4.8 | Event: request_made{streaming:false}; PR-28431 | Paula Mendes | parked | Risk: confounds retention measure; may be too subtle in 4 weeks.
C-06 | Console nudges | “View usage dashboard” nudge after first 10k tokens | Seeing spend/usage increases trust and continued use | D7 active; dashboard viewed rate | Activated users | 5 | 5 | 3 | 8.3 | Dashboard exists; ensure link stable | Marissa Cole | draft | Might also reduce support: “where are my tokens?” questions.
C-07 | Console | Email preferences link in settings + confirmation | Reduces complaints + improves deliverability by making preferences obvious | Complaint rate; unsub issues; support tickets | All users | 5 | 8 | 3 | 13.3 | ENG-10518; PR-28505 | Ethan Park | shipped | Shipped behind flag first; verify in EU flows for GDPR wording.

R-01 | Retargeting | Signup-no-activation audience (1–14 days) with dev-first creative + “Hello World” landing | Retargeting brings back stalled users and increases FSR | FSR within 7d of click; CAC/activated | Signup-no-activation | 7 | 5 | 6 | 5.8 | PM-670; audience defs; Jordan exclusions | Tyler Benson | in_progress | Strict suppression list required (enterprise domains + active opps). UTMs finalized 1/??.
R-02 | Retargeting | Site visitors (docs/quickstart) who didn’t signup (7 days) | Capture intent users and lift signup volume at acceptable CPA | Signup conversion; CPA signup | Anonymous visitors | 5 | 6 | 5 | 6.0 | PM-670; cookie consent constraints | Tyler Benson | queued | Check w/ Olga on EU consent & lookback windows. Might be ok only NA initially.
R-03 | Retargeting | Activated-but-no-return (activated in last 30d, no activity 7d) w/ “ship your first feature” examples | Bring back users after initial test drive → improve D30 | D30 active; cost per reactivated | Activated-no-return | 6 | 4 | 6 | 4.0 | PM-670; event pipeline reliability | Ben Carter | queued | Needs clear definition of “return” and exclude users who churned intentionally.
R-04 | Retargeting | Creative A/B: “Latency you can trust” vs “Lower $/token” | Identify strongest message for early-stage activation | CTR; activation per click | Retargeting cohorts | 5 | 7 | 3 | 11.7 | Creative kit DES-327 | Soojin Lee | queued | Keep claims tight (no hard numbers unless we can back up).
R-05 | Retargeting | Landing page A/B: quickstart vs console checklist | Better landing match increases conversion from click → FSR | FSR within 7d of click | Retargeting cohorts | 6 | 4 | 7 | 3.4 | Docs changes; console readiness | Marissa Cole | parked | Might be too many moving parts; do after we stabilize checklist.

M-01 | Measurement | Fix duplicate signup event emission + backfill sanity checks | Removes noise and improves confidence in experiment results | Event dedupe rate; funnel accuracy | All | 9 | 9 | 2 | 40.5 | PR-28521; ENG-10492 | Nadia Rahman | shipped | DONE. Need to re-run baseline funnel for last 14 days and annotate dashboard.
M-02 | Measurement | Standard event taxonomy + required properties for funnel | Consistent properties enable breakdowns (region, sdk, model) | % events with required props | All | 8 | 7 | 4 | 14.0 | ENG-10492; PR-28431 | Logan Wright | in_progress | Required props list: user_id, org_id, project_id, region, sdk, request_type, model.
M-03 | Measurement | Holdout group framework for nudges + email (randomized cohorts) | True holdout reduces false positives | Valid holdout assignment rate | New signups | 8 | 6 | 5 | 9.6 | PR-28477; ENG-10501 | Jada Williams | in_progress | Ensure cohort consistent across surfaces; watch for “multiple devices” issues.

Potentially correlate w/ support tickets tags.
