- Confirm which SSO/SCIM statements are safe for slides (Naomi + Elliot).
- Decide whether to show any specific audit log schema fields in slideware vs verbally only.
- Confirm whether we include a Marketplace segment (Partnerships) or keep it in Q&A.
- Finalize CTA priority: “security review call” vs “evidence pack request” vs “benchmark download.”
- Confirm lead routing rules (Grace + Tyler) so AEs get follow-up tasks within 24 hours.

Approvals needed before launch
- Slide claims review: Soojin + Naomi + Elliot
- Security posture statements: Dr. Aisha Rahman (final)
- Recording distribution + gating: Camila + Tyler

Notes to self (Ben)
- Keep the webinar practical: show logs/controls/ops, not just model choice.
- Avoid any statement that sounds like we’re offering compliance certification; anchor on “capabilities + evidence under NDA.”
- Ensure demo uses synthetic data and a clearly labeled “example environment.”

source.title: PLG Signals Pilot Cohort Analysis (last 30d self-serve) — usage distributions + cut lines
source.content:
Context / what this sheet is
- Goal: pick initial “sales-worthy” cut lines for the NA pilot (minimize spam, catch real scale/compliance intent).
- Cohort: self-serve orgs with >$200 total spend in last 30d OR >2M tokens total in last 30d.
- Time window: 2025-03-25 to 2025-04-23 (30d), with 7d deltas for velocity.
- Exclusions: internal/test orgs, sandbox workspaces, known agency/reseller accounts (tracked in allow/deny list; not fully clean yet).
- Notes: numbers below are directional (telemetry lag + billing reconciliation can shift spend by ~1–3%).

Data sources / definitions
- tokens_30d: total generated tokens (text+chat) in last 30d.
- tokens_day_avg_30d: tokens_30d / 30.
- spend_30d_usd: usage-based billed amount (net of free credits, pre-tax).
- spend_velocity_7d_wow: (spend last 7d) / (spend prior 7d). 1.0 = flat.
- req_429_rate_7d: 429 responses / total requests over last 7d (hosted API).
- p95_latency_ms_7d: p95 end-to-end latency for primary route(s), last 7d.
- intent_docs_score_14d (0–10): weighted count of “high-intent” doc events (Dedicated/Private docs, security FAQ, pricing/packaging pages) over 14d. (See working notes for exact weights.)
- workspaces_added_30d: new workspaces created under same org in last 30d.

Quick distribution summary (self-serve cohort, n=126)
Tokens/day (avg over last 30d)
- p50: 1.6M
- p75: 4.9M
- p90: 14.2M
- p95: 26.8M
- p99: 71.5M

Spend/30d (USD)
- p50: $620
- p75: $1,950
- p90: $6,900
- p95: $12,400
- p99: $31,800

429 rate (last 7d)
- p50: 0.02%
- p75: 0.08%
- p90: 0.35%
- p95: 0.85%
- p99: 2.40%

Intent docs score (last 14d)
- p50: 0
- p75: 1
- p90: 4
- p95: 6
- p99: 9

Proposed cut lines (MVP) — v0.9
(These are the “qualify into HubSpot task” thresholds; we can still log the underlying signal below this.)
A) Scale / throughput
- A1 “Sustained High Tokens”: tokens_day_avg_30d >= 25M AND active_days_14d >= 10
  - Rationale: catches p95+ usage; filters spiky one-day events.
- A2 “Spend Tier”: spend_30d_usd >= 10,000 OR (spend_30d_usd >= 5,000 AND spend_velocity_7d_wow >= 1.8)
  - Rationale: spend is a stronger proxy for expansion potential than tokens alone.

B) Reliability / rate limits
- B1 “Repeated 429s”: req_429_rate_7d >= 0.6% AND total_requests_7d >= 5,000
  - Rationale: avoid tiny denominators; 0.6% is ~p95.
- B2 “Latency Sensitivity”: p95_latency_ms_7d >= 1,800ms AND tokens_day_avg_30d >= 8M
  - Rationale: only route if they’re both meaningful volume and likely noticing.

C) Compliance / private intent
- C1 “Security/Compliance Intent”: intent_docs_score_14d >= 6 AND includes_security_faq_view=true
  - Rationale: p95 intent; security FAQ view is a strong qualifier.
- C2 “Private/Dedicated Docs Intent”: intent_docs_score_14d >= 7 AND includes_private_or_vpc_docs_view=true

D) Org expansion / multi-team
- D1 “Multi-workspace Expansion”: workspaces_added_30d >= 3 AND tokens_day_avg_30d >= 3M
  - Rationale: filters hacky workspace spam; requires some usage.

Suppression / guardrails (pilot)
- Suppress if: already has open opportunity OR is managed by CSM with active expansion plan OR “do_not_contact” flagged.
- Suppress repeat routing for same signal type within 14 days unless severity increases (e.g., tokens/day doubles OR 429 rate worsens by 2x).

Estimated weekly signal volume (based on last 30d cohort) — assuming cut lines above
- NA: 10–16 tasks/week (expected split: ~6 scale, ~4 reliability, ~3 compliance; overlap exists)
- EMEA: 4–7 tasks/week (higher compliance intent per account; lower overall spend)
- APAC: 2–4 tasks/week (more spiky usage; watch false positives)
- Total: ~16–27 tasks/week during pilot (before suppression + existing-owner preservation)

Top accounts (sample) — last 30d self-serve snapshot
Columns:
org_id | company | region | plan | tokens_30d | tokens_day_avg_30d | spend_30d_usd | spend_velocity_7d_wow | req_429_rate_7d | p95_latency_ms_7d | intent_docs_score_14d | workspaces_added_30d | proposed_signal(s) | qualify? | notes

10082 | HelioSearch | NA | paid-self-serve | 1,980,000,000 | 66,000,000 | 28,400 | 2.1 | 1.40% | 1,650 | 5 | 1 | A1,A2,B1 | YES | Clear Dedicated fit; repeated 429s + rapid spend velocity.
10411 | Arcade Robotics | NA | paid-self-serve | 840,000,000 | 28,000,000 | 12,100 | 1.6 | 0.22% | 2,050 | 3 | 2 | A1,B2 | YES | Latency sensitivity + sustained volume; SE-heavy (benchmarks).
10993 | Snowfield Media | EMEA | paid-self-serve | 515,000,000 | 17,166,667 | 8,900 | 1.9 | 0.10% | 1,120 | 7 | 1 | A2,C2 | YES | EU residency questions show up in docs trail.
10327 | Cascade Health | NA | paid-self-serve | 210,000,000 | 7,000,000 | 4,650 | 1.4 | 0.05% | 1,300 | 9 | 0 | C1,C2 | YES | Compliance-driven; likely Private/VPC conversation.
10114 | Ironbank Fintech | NA | paid-self-serve | 305,000,000 | 10,166,667 | 6,200 | 1.7 | 0.18% | 1,450 | 6 | 1 | A2,C1 | YES | Procurement motion likely; add DPA/retention notes.
11044 | Northpeak Legaltech | NA | paid-self-serve | 260,000,000 | 8,666,667 | 5,550 | 1.2 | 0.12% | 1,380 | 8 | 1 | C2 | YES | Private deployment docs + API key rotation events (qualitative).
10209 | Lumen Support Platform | NA | paid-self-serve | 145,000,000 | 4,833,333 | 2,700 | 2.4 | 0.08% | 1,020 | 4 | 5 | D1 | YES | Multi-team expansion; loop in CS to avoid conflicting outreach.
10688 | Quartz Commerce | NA | paid-self-serve | 92,000,000 | 3,066,667 | 1,950 | 1.1 | 0.04% | 980 | 7 | 0 | C1 | MAYBE | High intent score but low spend/usage; prior false positive risk.
10552 | VertexOps | NA | paid-self-serve | 420,000,000 | 14,000,000 | 7,800 | 2.0 | 0.72% | 1,910 | 2 | 1 | A2,B1,B2 | YES | Strong reliability pain; likely Dedicated for rate limiting.
11107 | Clarion Studio | EMEA | paid-self-serve | 120,000,000 | 4,000,000 | 3,150 | 2.2 | 0.05% | 1,040 | 6 | 0 | C1 | YES | Security FAQ + pricing page multiple times.
10719 | Medallion Maps | NA | paid-self-serve | 620,000,000 | 20,666,667 | 9,600 | 1.3 | 0.58% | 1,720 | 1 | 0 | A2 | YES | Just under A1; spend tier triggers; watch for 429 threshold.
11222 | Sable Analytics | NA | paid-self-serve | 75,000,000 | 2,500,000 | 1,120 | 3.0 | 0.02% | 890 | 5 | 0 | (none) | NO | Velocity high but absolute spend low; keep as log-only for now.
10801 | Papertrail CRM | NA | paid-self-serve | 310,000,000 | 10,333,333 | 4,980 | 1.9 | 0.10% | 1,980 | 2 | 0 | A2,B2 | YES | Latency pain; may need Dedicated or routing policy tuning.
11358 | Kestrel Video | APAC | paid-self-serve | 680,000,000 | 22,666,667 | 6,300 | 2.6 | 0.30% | 1,540 | 2 | 1 | A2 | YES | Spiky usage; ensure sustained-days check passes.
11402 | Brightline Edu | NA | paid-self-serve | 55,000,000 | 1,833,333 | 880 | 1.0 | 0.95% | 1,610 | 0 | 0 | B1 | MAYBE | 429s high but denominator small; verify total_requests_7d.
10910 | Driftwood Agents | NA | paid-self-serve | 1,050,000,000 | 35,000,000 | 11,700 | 1.2 | 0.40% | 2,220 | 1 | 0 | A1,B2 | YES | Pure scale + latency; good Dedicated benchmark candidate.
11577 | Harbor Insurance Tech | NA | paid-self-serve | 180,000,000 | 6,000,000 | 3,980 | 1.5 | 0.06% | 1,260 | 7 | 0 | C1 | YES | Security docs + SSO/SAML pages; likely compliance pack.
11631 | NovaCard | EMEA | paid-self-serve | 240,000,000 | 8,000,000 | 5,400 | 1.8 | 0.09% | 1,310 | 6 | 0 | C1,A2 | YES | Fintech + EU; route to EMEA owner.
11705 | Alloy Robotics | NA | paid-self-serve | 390,000,000 | 13,000,000 | 6,950 | 1.1 | 0.68% | 1,880 | 3 | 0 | B1,A2 | YES | 429 threshold crossed; likely needs Dedicated capacity.
11792 | Redwood Community Tooling (OSS) | NA | free | 240,000,000 | 8,000,000 | 0 | 0.0 | 0.00% | 1,050 | 0 | 0 | (exclude) | NO | Likely OSS demo / free credits; ensure deny list.
11844 | Orion Compliance | NA | paid-self-serve | 98,000,000 | 3,266,667 | 1,680 | 1.2 | 0.03% | 920 | 8 | 0 | C1,C2 | YES | Low spend but very high compliance intent; treat as SE/security assist.
11903 | FleetIQ | APAC | paid-self-serve | 510,000,000 | 17,000,000 | 4,900 | 2.2 | 0.15% | 1,470 | 2 | 3 | A2,D1 | YES | Org expansion + velocity; confirm this isn’t internal multi-env.
12066 | ScribeHealth | NA | paid-self-serve | 155,000,000 | 5,166,667 | 3,250 | 1.0 | 0.04% | 1,110 | 7 | 0 | C1 | YES | Similar to Cascade pattern; might be Private later.
12111 | Granite Retail AI | NA | paid-self-serve | 730,000,000 | 24,333,333 | 9,950 | 1.7 | 0.55% | 1,760 | 2 | 1 | A2 | YES | Right below A1; expect to cross next month.
12278 | Avrio Legal | EMEA | paid-self-serve | 62,000,000 | 2,066,667 | 1,150 | 1.3 | 0.02% | 840 | 6 | 0 | C1 | MAYBE | Might be “research-only” doc browsing; consider requiring pricing view.
12340 | Skyforge Games | NA | paid-self-serve | 910,000,000 | 30,333,333 | 8,200 | 3.4 | 0.20% | 2,600 | 1 | 0 | A1,A2,B2 | YES | Very fast ramp; likely needs capacity planning help.

Observations / open questions
- Most reliable “high quality” signals in this cohort are A2 (spend tier) and B1 (repeated 429s with denom guard).
- Compliance intent (C1/C2) triggers a smaller absolute number but seems high-conversion if routed correctly (SE + security pack).
- Quartz Commerce-style false positives appear when intent_docs_score is high but spend/tokens are low; suggestion: require either pricing view OR “security faq viewed on >=2 distinct days” to reduce noise.
- Workspaces_added_30d can be noisy (teams creating separate envs). We may want an “invited_users_30d” metric, but that’s not in the MVP contract yet.

Next actions
- [Ben/Tyler] confirm weekly task volume targets with Stephanie (pilot capacity: ~12 tasks/week for NA AEs).
- [Mei] sanity check spend reconciliation vs billing exports for top 20 accounts.
- [Isabelle] propose ownership + suppression rules for EMEA routing (residency/compliance signals).
- [Avery] spot-check top 10 “MAYBE” accounts for intent-only browsing vs real evaluation.

  - Rationale: spend is a stronger proxy for expansion potential than tokens alone.

B) Reliability / rate limits
- B1 “Repeated 429s”: req_429_rate_7d >= 0.6% AND total_requests_7d >= 5,000
  - Rationale: avoid tiny denominators; 0.6% is ~p95.
- B2 “Latency Sensitivity”: p95_latency_ms_7d >= 1,800ms AND tokens_day_avg_30d >= 8M
  - Rationale: only route if they’re both meaningful volume and likely noticing.

C) Compliance / private intent
- C1 “Security/Compliance Intent”: intent_docs_score_14d >= 6 AND includes_security_faq_view=true
  - Rationale: p95 intent; security FAQ view is a strong qualifier.
- C2 “Private/Dedicated Docs Intent”: intent_docs_score_14d >= 7 AND includes_private_or_vpc_docs_view=true

D) Org expansion / multi-team
- D1 “Multi-workspace Expansion”: workspaces_added_30d >= 3 AND tokens_day_avg_30d >= 3M
  - Rationale: filters hacky workspace spam; requires some usage.

Suppression / guardrails (pilot)
- Suppress if: already has open opportunity OR is managed by CSM with active expansion plan OR “do_not_contact” flagged.
- Suppress repeat routing for same signal type within 14 days unless severity increases (e.g., tokens/day doubles OR 429 rate worsens by 2x).

Estimated weekly signal volume (based on last 30d cohort) — assuming cut lines above
- NA: 10–16 tasks/week (expected split: ~6 scale, ~4 reliability, ~3 compliance; overlap exists)
- EMEA: 4–7 tasks/week (higher compliance intent per account; lower overall spend)
- APAC: 2–4 tasks/week (more spiky usage; watch false positives)
- Total: ~16–27 tasks/week during pilot (before suppression + existing-owner preservation)

Top accounts (sample) — last 30d self-serve snapshot
Columns:
org_id | company | region | plan | tokens_30d | tokens_day_avg_30d | spend_30d_usd | spend_velocity_7d_wow | req_429_rate_7d | p95_latency_ms_7d | intent_docs_score_14d | workspaces_added_30d | proposed_signal(s) | qualify? | notes

10082 | HelioSearch | NA | paid-self-serve | 1,980,000,000 | 66,000,000 | 28,400 | 2.1 | 1.40% | 1,650 | 5 | 1 | A1,A2,B1 | YES | Clear Dedicated fit; repeated 429s + rapid spend velocity.
10411 | Arcade Robotics | NA | paid-self-serve | 840,000,000 | 28,000,000 | 12,100 | 1.6 | 0.22% | 2,050 | 3 | 2 | A1,B2 | YES | Latency sensitivity + sustained volume; SE-heavy (benchmarks).
10993 | Snowfield Media | EMEA | paid-self-serve | 515,000,000 | 17,166,667 | 8,900 | 1.9 | 0.10% | 1,120 | 7 | 1 | A2,C2 | YES | EU residency questions show up in docs trail.
10327 | Cascade Health | NA | paid-self-serve | 210,000,000 | 7,000,000 | 4,650 | 1.4 | 0.05% | 1,300 | 9 | 0 | C1,C2 | YES | Compliance-driven; likely Private/VPC conversation.
10114 | Ironbank Fintech | NA | paid-self-serve | 305,000,000 | 10,166,667 | 6,200 | 1.7 | 0.18% | 1,450 | 6 | 1 | A2,C1 | YES | Procurement motion likely; add DPA/retention notes.
11044 | Northpeak Legaltech | NA | paid-self-serve | 260,000,000 | 8,666,667 | 5,550 | 1.2 | 0.12% | 1,380 | 8 | 1 | C2 | YES | Private deployment docs + API key rotation events (qualitative).
10209 | Lumen Support Platform | NA | paid-self-serve | 145,000,000 | 4,833,333 | 2,700 | 2.4 | 0.08% | 1,020 | 4 | 5 | D1 | YES | Multi-team expansion; loop in CS to avoid conflicting outreach.
10688 | Quartz Commerce | NA | paid-self-serve | 92,000,000 | 3,066,667 | 1,950 | 1.1 | 0.04% | 980 | 7 | 0 | C1 | MAYBE | High intent score but low spend/usage; prior false positive risk.
10552 | VertexOps | NA | paid-self-serve | 420,000,000 | 14,000,000 | 7,800 | 2.0 | 0.72% | 1,910 | 2 | 1 | A2,B1,B2 | YES | Strong reliability pain; likely Dedicated for rate limiting.
11107 | Clarion Studio | EMEA | paid-self-serve | 120,000,000 | 4,000,000 | 3,150 | 2.2 | 0.05% | 1,040 | 6 | 0 | C1 | YES | Security FAQ + pricing page multiple times.
10719 | Medallion Maps | NA | paid-self-serve | 620,000,000 | 20,666,667 | 9,600 | 1.3 | 0.58% | 1,720 | 1 | 0 | A2 | YES | Just under A1; spend tier triggers; watch for 429 threshold.
11222 | Sable Analytics | NA | paid-self-serve | 75,000,000 | 2,500,000 | 1,120 | 3.0 | 0.02% | 890 | 5 | 0 | (none) | NO | Velocity high but absolute spend low; keep as log-only for now.
10801 | Papertrail CRM | NA | paid-self-serve | 310,000,000 | 10,333,333 | 4,980 | 1.9 | 0.10% | 1,980 | 2 | 0 | A2,B2 | YES | Latency pain; may need Dedicated or routing policy tuning.
11358 | Kestrel Video | APAC | paid-self-serve | 680,000,000 | 22,666,667 | 6,300 | 2.6 | 0.30% | 1,540 | 2 | 1 | A2 | YES | Spiky usage; ensure sustained-days check passes.
11402 | Brightline Edu | NA | paid-self-serve | 55,000,000 | 1,833,333 | 880 | 1.0 | 0.95% | 1,610 | 0 | 0 | B1 | MAYBE | 429s high but denominator small; verify total_requests_7d.
10910 | Driftwood Agents | NA | paid-self-serve | 1,050,000,000 | 35,000,000 | 11,700 | 1.2 | 0.40% | 2,220 | 1 | 0 | A1,B2 | YES | Pure scale + latency; good Dedicated benchmark candidate.
11577 | Harbor Insurance Tech | NA | paid-self-serve | 180,000,000 | 6,000,000 | 3,980 | 1.5 | 0.06% | 1,260 | 7 | 0 | C1 | YES | Security docs + SSO/SAML pages; likely compliance pack.
11631 | NovaCard | EMEA | paid-self-serve | 240,000,000 | 8,000,000 | 5,400 | 1.8 | 0.09% | 1,310 | 6 | 0 | C1,A2 | YES | Fintech + EU; route to EMEA owner.
11705 | Alloy Robotics | NA | paid-self-serve | 390,000,000 | 13,000,000 | 6,950 | 1.1 | 0.68% | 1,880 | 3 | 0 | B1,A2 | YES | 429 threshold crossed; likely needs Dedicated capacity.
11792 | Redwood Community Tooling (OSS) | NA | free | 240,000,000 | 8,000,000 | 0 | 0.0 | 0.00% | 1,050 | 0 | 0 | (exclude) | NO | Likely OSS demo / free credits; ensure deny list.
11844 | Orion Compliance | NA | paid-self-serve | 98,000,000 | 3,266,667 | 1,680 | 1.2 | 0.03% | 920 | 8 | 0 | C1,C2 | YES | Low spend but very high compliance intent; treat as SE/security assist.
11903 | FleetIQ | APAC | paid-self-serve | 510,000,000 | 17,000,000 | 4,900 | 2.2 | 0.15% | 1,470 | 2 | 3 | A2,D1 | YES | Org expansion + velocity; confirm this isn’t internal multi-env.
12066 | ScribeHealth | NA | paid-self-serve | 155,000,000 | 5,166,667 | 3,250 | 1.0 | 0.04% | 1,110 | 7 | 0 | C1 | YES | Similar to Cascade pattern; might be Private later.
12111 | Granite Retail AI | NA | paid-self-serve | 730,000,000 | 24,333,333 | 9,950 | 1.7 | 0.55% | 1,760 | 2 | 1 | A2 | YES | Right below A1; expect to cross next month.
12278 | Avrio Legal | EMEA | paid-self-serve | 62,000,000 | 2,066,667 | 1,150 | 1.3 | 0.02% | 840 | 6 | 0 | C1 | MAYBE | Might be “research-only” doc browsing; consider requiring pricing view.
12340 | Skyforge Games | NA | paid-self-serve | 910,000,000 | 30,333,333 | 8,200 | 3.4 | 0.20% | 2,600 | 1 | 0 | A1,A2,B2 | YES | Very fast ramp; likely needs capacity planning help.

Observations / open questions
- Most reliable “high quality” signals in this cohort are A2 (spend tier) and B1 (repeated 429s with denom guard).
- Compliance intent (C1/C2) triggers a smaller absolute number but seems high-conversion if routed correctly (SE + security pack).
- Quartz Commerce-style false positives appear when intent_docs_score is high but spend/tokens are low; suggestion: require either pricing view OR “security faq viewed on >=2 distinct days” to reduce noise.
- Workspaces_added_30d can be noisy (teams creating separate envs). We may want an “invited_users_30d” metric, but that’s not in the MVP contract yet.

Next actions
- [Ben/Tyler] confirm weekly task volume targets with Stephanie (pilot capacity: ~12 tasks/week for NA AEs).
- [Mei] sanity check spend reconciliation vs billing exports for top 20 accounts.
- [Isabelle] propose ownership + suppression rules for EMEA routing (residency/compliance signals).
- [Avery] spot-check top 10 “MAYBE” accounts for intent-only browsing vs real evaluation.

source.title: PLG→Sales signals: taxonomy + candidate thresholds (working notes)
source.content:
Last updated: 2025-05-27 (Ben)

Purpose
- Brainstorm + converge on a canonical list of “sales-worthy” PLG signals for Hosted → Dedicated / Private upgrades.
- Propose initial thresholds for NA pilot; expect tuning after week 1–2.
- Capture open questions + early stakeholder interview notes.

Non-goals (for this doc)
- Final scoring model (that will live in ADR / PRD)
- Final HubSpot workflows, task templates

Glossary / framing
- Signal = an aggregated, privacy-aware indicator of (a) scale, (b) pain (reliability/cost), or (c) intent (upgrade/compliance).
- We should separate:
  1) Threshold signals (“they’re big / growing”) and
  2) Intent signals (“they’re looking for Dedicated/Private/security/pricing”).
- Ideal signal qualities:
  - actionability (AE/SE can do something within 24–48 hours)
  - specificity (not just “they used the product”)
  - low spam: avoid triggering on single-page curiosity / random browsing
  - privacy-safe: no prompts, no user message content; mostly aggregates + doc page IDs

Primary motions we’re trying to unlock
1) Hosted → Dedicated
- sustained throughput needs
- repeated rate limiting (429) / capacity constraints
- latency sensitivity / p95 targets
- desire for predictable spend + SLA-ish comfort

2) Hosted → Private (VPC/on-prem)
- regulated customer (healthcare/fintech/legal)
- data residency / customer-managed networking
- audit logs, SSO/SAML, KMS/HSM, retention controls

Candidate signal taxonomy (v0.3)
A) Scale / Growth (threshold)
B) Reliability / SLO pain (threshold + “pain”)
C) Cost / Spend velocity (threshold)
D) Compliance & Security (intent)
E) Product intent (Dedicated/Private docs, pricing, console paths)
F) Org expansion (multi-workspace, invites, teams)
G) Procurement / enterprise behavior (domain, buyer-ish behaviors) — TBD, likely later

Candidate signals + strawman thresholds (pilot starting point)
Notes:
- Most thresholds below are intentionally conservative. Early pilot feedback: too much volume is worse than missing some.
- We need different handling for trial vs paid (see open questions).

Table: candidate signals (draft)

1) Scale / throughput
- S1: Sustained token volume (30d)
  - Definition: total_tokens_30d >= 300M OR avg_tokens_per_day_30d >= 10M
  - Rationale: this is where Dedicated usually pencils out + capacity planning matters
  - Caveat: embeddings-heavy orgs (tokens not always best proxy)

- S2: Sustained RPS / concurrency proxy
  - Definition: requests_7d >= 1.5M OR peak_rpm_7d >= 25k (needs telemetry availability)
  - Rationale: indicates operational pressure even if tokens moderate
  - Caveat: we don’t always have clean peak metrics; may require percentile approximations

- S3: Context length skew / long context usage
  - Definition: p95_input_tokens_7d >= 8k AND total_requests_7d >= 50k
  - Rationale: long context drives cost + latency; Dedicated optimization story is stronger
  - Caveat: risk of noisy; requires accurate tokenization metrics per request

2) Reliability / pain
- R1: Repeated 429s (rate limited)
  - Definition: http_429_count_7d >= 200 AND http_429_rate_7d >= 0.5%
  - Alternate: >= 50 per day for 3+ days
  - Action: “we can help with Dedicated capacity / burst / tuning”
  - Guardrail: ignore if all 429s occur in < 2 hours (script gone wild)

- R2: Elevated error rate (non-429)
  - Definition: (5xx + timeouts)_7d >= 100 AND error_rate_7d >= 0.3%
  - Action: SE or Support-involved; do not do sales-y email if incident ongoing
  - Guardrail: suppression if Support ticket open (need integration; otherwise manual)

- R3: Latency regression / p95 above threshold
  - Definition: p95_latency_ms_7d >= 2500ms AND requests_7d >= 100k
  - Action: performance tuning + Dedicated pitch if they have strict SLOs
  - Note: latency is workload-dependent; be careful with a single global line

3) Cost / spend velocity
- C1: Spend velocity spike
  - Definition: spend_7d >= $7,500 AND spend_7d >= 1.5 * spend_prev_7d
  - Alternate: spend_30d >= $25,000
  - Action: “predictable pricing, reserved capacity, volume economics”

- C2: Unit economics stress (if we can compute)
  - Definition: cost_per_successful_request_7d up > 20% WoW AND volume stable
  - Risk: likely later; needs careful instrumentation to avoid false positives

4) Compliance & security intent
- S4: Security FAQ / compliance doc engagement
  - Definition: >= 3 unique visits to any security/compliance doc pages within 14d AND time_on_page >= 45s
  - Stronger: security pages + “SOC2” keyword search event OR “DPA” request event (if tracked)
  - Action: route to SE + AE; use security talk track; offer evidence pack
  - Guardrail: filter internal IPs + known vendor audits

- S5: Private / VPC docs intent
  - Definition: >= 2 visits to Private/VPC/on-prem docs in 7d OR clicked “VPC” from pricing page
  - Action: SE involvement required; qualify for network constraints, data residency

- S6: SSO/SAML / KMS / audit logs docs intent
  - Definition: >= 2 visits to enterprise security feature docs in 7d
  - Note: should contribute to score vs standalone trigger (to avoid doc browsers)

5) Dedicated product intent
- I1: Dedicated docs engagement
  - Definition: >= 2 visits to Dedicated docs in 7d OR dedicated_overview page + pricing page within 48h
  - Action: AE outreach with “capacity + performance + cost” messaging

- I2: Console actions suggesting scaling
  - Definition: visited “rate limits” view OR “usage analytics” view >= 3 times in 7d
  - Action: SE light touch or email; could be a “help” signal rather than pure sales
  - Risk: may correlate with confusion; ensure tone is support-first

6) Org expansion
- O1: New workspaces created
  - Definition: net_new_workspaces_14d >= 2
  - Action: position standardization + governance; involve CS for existing paid accounts

- O2: Invite spike / new seats proxy
  - Definition: new_org_members_14d >= 10
  - Action: ask about internal rollout; security/compliance + Dedicated often follow

7) Upgrade friction / operational maturity (possible later)
- F1: API key rotation / multiple keys created
  - Definition: api_keys_created_30d >= 5 OR api_keys_rotated_30d >= 2
  - Hypothesis: indicates larger team / security posture
  - Risk: could be a red flag (bad key hygiene) not a sales opportunity

- F2: Multiple environments / projects
  - Definition: “prod” + “staging” tag usage in 14d (if tracked)
  - TBD

Signal strength tiers (draft)
- Tier A (route immediately to AE + task):
  - Repeated 429s + high volume
  - Private/VPC intent
  - Security/compliance intent + sufficient account size (see below)
  - Spend velocity above threshold

- Tier B (add to HubSpot as signal history; create task only if combined score >= X):
  - Dedicated docs views alone
  - Latency issues without size confirmation
  - Console analytics interest
  - Org expansion signals

- Tier C (do not route in pilot; log only):
  - Single doc view
  - pricing page view only
  - “curiosity signals” without size/intent corroboration

Trial vs paid handling (open topic)
- Problem: trials can generate large token bursts (eval runs) but low conversion to sales.
- Proposal v0:
  - If account is trial/free:
    - require intent + threshold OR two independent intent categories
    - use softer outreach template ("want help benchmarking / best practices")
  - If paid self-serve:
    - threshold-only triggers allowed (spend velocity / 429s) + clear upgrade CTA
- Data question: what is the canonical “plan status” field in telemetry dataset vs billing?

Noise / false positive suppression ideas (early)
- Global suppression windows:
  - no more than 1 outbound task per company per 14 days unless Tier A
  - do not re-trigger the same signal type within 30 days unless severity increases by 2x

- Exclusions / denylist:
  - internal Redwood orgs + known test org patterns
  - known partners / vendors doing due diligence (need list from Partnerships)

- “Do not outreach” guardrails:
  - active support incident or P0 outage window (if detectable)
  - customer explicitly opted out of sales outreach (need to respect HubSpot field)

- Combine signals to reduce spam:
  - docs intent should usually require either (a) time-on-page threshold OR (b) repeated visits OR (c) paired with pricing page within 48h

Scoring sketch (very rough)
- Total score = scale_points + pain_points + intent_points + expansion_points
- Example weights (subject to change):
  - Spend velocity (C1): +6
  - Repeated 429s (R1): +5
  - Private/VPC intent (S5): +6
  - Security FAQ intent (S4): +4
  - Dedicated docs intent (I1): +3
  - Org expansion (O1/O2): +2
  - Size (S1): +4
- Routing threshold idea:
  - Create AE task if score >= 8, OR any Tier A single signal fires.

Stakeholder interview notes (early, paraphrased)

Stephanie (VP Sales NA) — 2025-04-30
- “I don’t want 50 tasks a day. Give me 5–10 high-confidence and we’ll actually work them.”
- Biggest wins historically: (1) 429 pain + growth and (2) compliance signals from healthcare/fintech.
- Ownership: preserve existing owner always; otherwise NA round-robin.

Markus (SE lead) — 2025-05-01
- For Dedicated: “429s + sustained volume is the cleanest ‘we can fix this’ story.”
- For Private: “Docs views are good, but only if paired with a concrete requirement (VPC, residency, audit logs).”
- SE involvement should be automatic for Private/VPC intent and for latency/SLO claims.

David (HubSpot admin) — 2025-05-02
- HubSpot rate limits are real. Avoid 10 property updates per signal; batch + idempotency.
- Keep the properties minimal for MVP: last_signal_type, last_signal_date, score, top_reasons, next_task_due.

Tyler (Marketing Ops) — 2025-05-03
- Avoid conflicting touches with lifecycle nurture.
- Suggest: when AE task created, suppress marketing “upgrade” email for 7–10 days.

Jasmine (CS lead) — 2025-05-06
- For existing paying customers, CS should be informed before outreach that changes pricing/architecture.
- “Expansion vs escalation” confusion risk: if errors are high, it might be a support issue.

Dr. Aisha (Security/Privacy) — 2025-05-08
- Keep payloads going into HubSpot privacy-minimal.
- Don’t export raw URLs with query params if they contain identifiers; prefer page IDs / categories.
- Retention: signals should have TTL; don’t keep granular browsing events indefinitely.

Open questions / TODO
1) Data availability
- Do we have reliable unique doc page view events tied to company/workspace? (Elliot + Docs instrumentation)
- Can we compute p95 latency by company without high cost / sampling issues?

2) Threshold calibration
- Need distribution cut lines from cohort analysis sheet (tokens/day, spend, 429 rate).
- How many signals/week should pilot produce per region? Target: ~25–40/week total for NA.

3) False positives
- Known example pattern: “pricing + security FAQ” from small agencies who are just comparing vendors.
- How do we distinguish “vendor research” vs “active evaluation”? Maybe require product usage above minimum.

4) Trial vs paid
- Confirm plan status source of truth + how quickly it updates.
- Do we treat startups on free credits as “trial”? (likely yes)

5) Ownership + handoff
- If an org already has a CSM owner (Dedicated/Private), who owns the task? CS-first or AE-first?

6) Messaging
- Need templates that are support-first for reliability signals (“we noticed rate limiting; want help tuning/burst?”) vs sales-first.

Next steps
- Align taxonomy + initial thresholds with Nikhil (PRD owner) + Stephanie (NA pilot) by 2025-05-09.
- Pull top 30d cohort stats and estimate weekly volume at each cut line.
- Decide Tier A triggers for MVP (suggest: R1, C1, S5, S4+size).
- Document suppression rules explicitly (to reduce spam + avoid SUP tickets).

Change log
- 2025-05-12: added time-on-page + repeated-visit guardrails for intent docs.
- 2025-05-19: raised 429 threshold proposal; added “short burst” suppression idea.
- 2025-05-27: added trial vs paid split + marketing suppression note; clarified Tier A vs Tier B.

Candidate signals + strawman thresholds (pilot starting point)
Notes:
- Most thresholds below are intentionally conservative. Early pilot feedback: too much volume is worse than missing some.
- We need different handling for trial vs paid (see open questions).

Table: candidate signals (draft)

1) Scale / throughput

  - Rationale: this is where Dedicated usually pencils out + capacity planning matters

  - Caveat: we don’t always have clean peak metrics; may require percentile approximations

- S3: Context length skew / long context usage
  - Definition: p95_input_tokens_7d >= 8k AND total_requests_7d >= 50k
  - Rationale: long context drives cost + latency; Dedicated optimization story is stronger
  - Caveat: risk of noisy; requires accurate tokenization metrics per request

2) Reliability / pain
- R1: Repeated 429s (rate limited)
  - Definition: http_429_count_7d >= 200 AND http_429_rate_7d >= 0.5%

- R2: Elevated error rate (non-429)

- R3: Latency regression / p95 above threshold

  - Action: performance tuning + Dedicated pitch if they have strict SLOs
  - Note: latency is workload-dependent; be careful with a single global line

3) Cost / spend velocity
- C1: Spend velocity spike
  - Definition: spend_7d >= $7,500 AND spend_7d >= 1.5 * spend_prev_7d
  - Alternate: spend_30d >= $25,000
  - Action: “predictable pricing, reserved capacity, volume economics”

- C2: Unit economics stress (if we can compute)
  - Definition: cost_per_successful_request_7d up > 20% WoW AND volume stable
  - Risk: likely later; needs careful instrumentation to avoid false positives

4) Compliance & security intent
- S4: Security FAQ / compliance doc engagement
  - Definition: >= 3 unique visits to any security/compliance doc pages within 14d AND time_on_page >= 45s
  - Stronger: security pages + “SOC2” keyword search event OR “DPA” request event (if tracked)
  - Action: route to SE + AE; use security talk track; offer evidence pack
  - Guardrail: filter internal IPs + known vendor audits

- S5: Private / VPC docs intent
  - Definition: >= 2 visits to Private/VPC/on-prem docs in 7d OR clicked “VPC” from pricing page
  - Action: SE involvement required; qualify for network constraints, data residency

- S6: SSO/SAML / KMS / audit logs docs intent
  - Definition: >= 2 visits to enterprise security feature docs in 7d
  - Note: should contribute to score vs standalone trigger (to avoid doc browsers)

5) Dedicated product intent
- I1: Dedicated docs engagement
  - Definition: >= 2 visits to Dedicated docs in 7d OR dedicated_overview page + pricing page within 48h
  - Action: AE outreach with “capacity + performance + cost” messaging

- I2: Console actions suggesting scaling
  - Definition: visited “rate limits” view OR “usage analytics” view >= 3 times in 7d
  - Action: SE light touch or email; could be a “help” signal rather than pure sales
  - Risk: may correlate with confusion; ensure tone is support-first

6) Org expansion
- O1: New workspaces created
  - Definition: net_new_workspaces_14d >= 2
  - Action: position standardization + governance; involve CS for existing paid accounts

- O2: Invite spike / new seats proxy
  - Definition: new_org_members_14d >= 10
  - Action: ask about internal rollout; security/compliance + Dedicated often follow

7) Upgrade friction / operational maturity (possible later)
- F1: API key rotation / multiple keys created
  - Definition: api_keys_created_30d >= 5 OR api_keys_rotated_30d >= 2
  - Hypothesis: indicates larger team / security posture
  - Risk: could be a red flag (bad key hygiene) not a sales opportunity

- F2: Multiple environments / projects
  - Definition: “prod” + “staging” tag usage in 14d (if tracked)
  - TBD

Signal strength tiers (draft)
- Tier A (route immediately to AE + task):
  - Repeated 429s + high volume
  - Private/VPC intent
  - Security/compliance intent + sufficient account size (see below)
  - Spend velocity above threshold

- Tier B (add to HubSpot as signal history; create task only if combined score >= X):
  - Dedicated docs views alone
  - Latency issues without size confirmation
  - Console analytics interest
  - Org expansion signals

- Tier C (do not route in pilot; log only):
  - Single doc view
  - pricing page view only
  - “curiosity signals” without size/intent corroboration

Trial vs paid handling (open topic)
- Problem: trials can generate large token bursts (eval runs) but low conversion to sales.
- Proposal v0:
  - If account is trial/free:
    - require intent + threshold OR two independent intent categories
    - use softer outreach template ("want help benchmarking / best practices")
  - If paid self-serve:
    - threshold-only triggers allowed (spend velocity / 429s) + clear upgrade CTA
- Data question: what is the canonical “plan status” field in telemetry dataset vs billing?

Noise / false positive suppression ideas (early)
- Global suppression windows:
  - no more than 1 outbound task per company per 14 days unless Tier A
  - do not re-trigger the same signal type within 30 days unless severity increases by 2x

- Exclusions / denylist:
  - internal Redwood orgs + known test org patterns
  - known partners / vendors doing due diligence (need list from Partnerships)

- “Do not outreach” guardrails:
  - active support incident or P0 outage window (if detectable)
  - customer explicitly opted out of sales outreach (need to respect HubSpot field)

- Combine signals to reduce spam:
  - docs intent should usually require either (a) time-on-page threshold OR (b) repeated visits OR (c) paired with pricing page within 48h

Scoring sketch (very rough)
- Total score = scale_points + pain_points + intent_points + expansion_points
- Example weights (subject to change):
  - Spend velocity (C1): +6
  - Repeated 429s (R1): +5
  - Private/VPC intent (S5): +6
  - Security FAQ intent (S4): +4
  - Dedicated docs intent (I1): +3
  - Org expansion (O1/O2): +2
  - Size (S1): +4
- Routing threshold idea:
  - Create AE task if score >= 8, OR any Tier A single signal fires.

Stakeholder interview notes (early, paraphrased)

Stephanie (VP Sales NA) — 2025-04-30
- “I don’t want 50 tasks a day. Give me 5–10 high-confidence and we’ll actually work them.”
- Biggest wins historically: (1) 429 pain + growth and (2) compliance signals from healthcare/fintech.
- Ownership: preserve existing owner always; otherwise NA round-robin.

Markus (SE lead) — 2025-05-01
- For Dedicated: “429s + sustained volume is the cleanest ‘we can fix this’ story.”
- For Private: “Docs views are good, but only if paired with a concrete requirement (VPC, residency, audit logs).”
- SE involvement should be automatic for Private/VPC intent and for latency/SLO claims.

David (HubSpot admin) — 2025-05-02
- HubSpot rate limits are real. Avoid 10 property updates per signal; batch + idempotency.
- Keep the properties minimal for MVP: last_signal_type, last_signal_date, score, top_reasons, next_task_due.

Tyler (Marketing Ops) — 2025-05-03
- Avoid conflicting touches with lifecycle nurture.
- Suggest: when AE task created, suppress marketing “upgrade” email for 7–10 days.

Jasmine (CS lead) — 2025-05-06
- For existing paying customers, CS should be informed before outreach that changes pricing/architecture.
- “Expansion vs escalation” confusion risk: if errors are high, it might be a support issue.

Dr. Aisha (Security/Privacy) — 2025-05-08
- Keep payloads going into HubSpot privacy-minimal.
- Don’t export raw URLs with query params if they contain identifiers; prefer page IDs / categories.
- Retention: signals should have TTL; don’t keep granular browsing events indefinitely.

Open questions / TODO
1) Data availability
- Do we have reliable unique doc page view events tied to company/workspace? (Elliot + Docs instrumentation)
- Can we compute p95 latency by company without high cost / sampling issues?

2) Threshold calibration
- Need distribution cut lines from cohort analysis sheet (tokens/day, spend, 429 rate).
- How many signals/week should pilot produce per region? Target: ~25–40/week total for NA.

3) False positives
- Known example pattern: “pricing + security FAQ” from small agencies who are just comparing vendors.
- How do we distinguish “vendor research” vs “active evaluation”? Maybe require product usage above minimum.

4) Trial vs paid
- Confirm plan status source of truth + how quickly it updates.
- Do we treat startups on free credits as “trial”? (likely yes)

5) Ownership + handoff
- If an org already has a CSM owner (Dedicated/Private), who owns the task? CS-first or AE-first?

6) Messaging
- Need templates that are support-first for reliability signals (“we noticed rate limiting; want help tuning/burst?”) vs sales-first.

Next steps
- Align taxonomy + initial thresholds with Nikhil (PRD owner) + Stephanie (NA pilot) by 2025-05-09.
- Pull top 30d cohort stats and estimate weekly volume at each cut line.
- Decide Tier A triggers for MVP (suggest: R1, C1, S5, S4+size).
- Document suppression rules explicitly (to reduce spam + avoid SUP tickets).

Change log
- 2025-05-12: added time-on-page + repeated-visit guardrails for intent docs.
- 2025-05-19: raised 429 threshold proposal; added “short burst” suppression idea.
- 2025-05-27: added trial vs paid split + marketing suppression note; clarified Tier A vs Tier B.
text:
Last updated: 2025-05-27 (Ben)

Purpose
- Brainstorm + converge on a canonical list of “sales-worthy” PLG signals for Hosted → Dedicated / Private upgrades.
- Propose initial thresholds for NA pilot; expect tuning after week 1–2.
- Capture open questions + early stakeholder interview notes.

Non-goals (for this doc)
- Final scoring model (that will live in ADR / PRD)
- Final HubSpot workflows, task templates

Glossary / framing
- Signal = an aggregated, privacy-aware indicator of (a) scale, (b) pain (reliability/cost), or (c) intent (upgrade/compliance).
- We should separate:
  1) Threshold signals (“they’re big / growing”) and
  2) Intent signals (“they’re looking for Dedicated/Private/security/pricing”).
- Ideal signal qualities:
  - actionability (AE/SE can do something within 24–48 hours)
  - specificity (not just “they used the product”)
  - low spam: avoid triggering on single-page curiosity / random browsing
  - privacy-safe: no prompts, no user message content; mostly aggregates + doc page IDs

Primary motions we’re trying to unlock
1) Hosted → Dedicated
- sustained throughput needs
- repeated rate limiting (429) / capacity constraints
- latency sensitivity / p95 targets
- desire for predictable spend + SLA-ish comfort

2) Hosted → Private (VPC/on-prem)
- regulated customer (healthcare/fintech/legal)
- data residency / customer-managed networking
- audit logs, SSO/SAML, KMS/HSM, retention controls

Candidate signal taxonomy (v0.3)
A) Scale / Growth (threshold)
B) Reliability / SLO pain (threshold + “pain”)
C) Cost / Spend velocity (threshold)
D) Compliance & Security (intent)
E) Product intent (Dedicated/Private docs, pricing, console paths)
F) Org expansion (multi-workspace, invites, teams)
G) Procurement / enterprise behavior (domain, buyer-ish behaviors) — TBD, likely later

Candidate signals + strawman thresholds (pilot starting point)
Notes:
- Most thresholds below are intentionally conservative. Early pilot feedback: too much volume is worse than missing some.
- We need different handling for trial vs paid (see open questions).

Table: candidate signals (draft)

1) Scale / throughput
- S1: Sustained token volume (30d)
  - Definition: total_tokens_30d >= 300M OR avg_tokens_per_day_30d >= 10M
  - Rationale: this is where Dedicated usually pencils out + capacity planning matters
  - Caveat: embeddings-heavy orgs (tokens not always best proxy)

- S2: Sustained RPS / concurrency proxy
  - Definition: requests_7d >= 1.5M OR peak_rpm_7d >= 25k (needs telemetry availability)
  - Rationale: indicates operational pressure even if tokens moderate
  - Caveat: we don’t always have clean peak metrics; may require percentile approximations

- S3: Context length skew / long context usage
  - Definition: p95_input_tokens_7d >= 8k AND total_requests_7d >= 50k
  - Rationale: long context drives cost + latency; Dedicated optimization story is stronger
  - Caveat: risk of noisy; requires accurate tokenization metrics per request

2) Reliability / pain
- R1: Repeated 429s (rate limited)
  - Definition: http_429_count_7d >= 200 AND http_429_rate_7d >= 0.5%
  - Alternate: >= 50 per day for 3+ days
  - Action: “we can help with Dedicated capacity / burst / tuning”
  - Guardrail: ignore if all 429s occur in < 2 hours (script gone wild)

- R2: Elevated error rate (non-429)
  - Definition: (5xx + timeouts)_7d >= 100 AND error_rate_7d >= 0.3%
  - Action: SE or Support-involved; do not do sales-y email if incident ongoing
  - Guardrail: suppression if Support ticket open (need integration; otherwise manual)

- R3: Latency regression / p95 above threshold
  - Definition: p95_latency_ms_7d >= 2500ms AND requests_7d >= 100k
  - Action: performance tuning + Dedicated pitch if they have strict SLOs
  - Note: latency is workload-dependent; be careful with a single global line

3) Cost / spend velocity
- C1: Spend velocity spike
  - Definition: spend_7d >= $7,500 AND spend_7d >= 1.5 * spend_prev_7d
  - Alternate: spend_30d >= $25,000
  - Action: “predictable pricing, reserved capacity, volume economics”

- C2: Unit economics stress (if we can compute)
  - Definition: cost_per_successful_request_7d up > 20% WoW AND volume stable
  - Risk: likely later; needs careful instrumentation to avoid false positives

4) Compliance & security intent
- S4: Security FAQ / compliance doc engagement
  - Definition: >= 3 unique visits to any security/compliance doc pages within 14d AND time_on_page >= 45s
  - Stronger: security pages + “SOC2” keyword search event OR “DPA” request event (if tracked)
  - Action: route to SE + AE; use security talk track; offer evidence pack
  - Guardrail: filter internal IPs + known vendor audits

- S5: Private / VPC docs intent
  - Definition: >= 2 visits to Private/VPC/on-prem docs in 7d OR clicked “VPC” from pricing page
  - Action: SE involvement required; qualify for network constraints, data residency

- S6: SSO/SAML / KMS / audit logs docs intent
  - Definition: >= 2 visits to enterprise security feature docs in 7d
  - Note: should contribute to score vs standalone trigger (to avoid doc browsers)

5) Dedicated product intent
- I1: Dedicated docs engagement
  - Definition: >= 2 visits to Dedicated docs in 7d OR dedicated_overview page + pricing page within 48h
  - Action: AE outreach with “capacity + performance + cost” messaging

- I2: Console actions suggesting scaling
  - Definition: visited “rate limits” view OR “usage analytics” view >= 3 times in 7d
  - Action: SE light touch or email; could be a “help” signal rather than pure sales
  - Risk: may correlate with confusion; ensure tone is support-first

6) Org expansion
- O1: New workspaces created
  - Definition: net_new_workspaces_14d >= 2
  - Action: position standardization + governance; involve CS for existing paid accounts

- O2: Invite spike / new seats proxy
  - Definition: new_org_members_14d >= 10
  - Action: ask about internal rollout; security/compliance + Dedicated often follow

7) Upgrade friction / operational maturity (possible later)
- F1: API key rotation / multiple keys created
  - Definition: api_keys_created_30d >= 5 OR api_keys_rotated_30d >= 2
  - Hypothesis: indicates larger team / security posture
  - Risk: could be a red flag (bad key hygiene) not a sales opportunity

- F2: Multiple environments / projects
  - Definition: “prod” + “staging” tag usage in 14d (if tracked)
  - TBD

Signal strength tiers (draft)
- Tier A (route immediately to AE + task):
  - Repeated 429s + high volume
  - Private/VPC intent
  - Security/compliance intent + sufficient account size (see below)
  - Spend velocity above threshold

- Tier B (add to HubSpot as signal history; create task only if combined score >= X):
  - Dedicated docs views alone
  - Latency issues without size confirmation
  - Console analytics interest
  - Org expansion signals

- Tier C (do not route in pilot; log only):
  - Single doc view
  - pricing page view only
  - “curiosity signals” without size/intent corroboration

Trial vs paid handling (open topic)
- Problem: trials can generate large token bursts (eval runs) but low conversion to sales.
- Proposal v0:
  - If account is trial/free:
    - require intent + threshold OR two independent intent categories
    - use softer outreach template ("want help benchmarking / best practices")
  - If paid self-serve:
    - threshold-only triggers allowed (spend velocity / 429s) + clear upgrade CTA
- Data question: what is the canonical “plan status” field in telemetry dataset vs billing?

Noise / false positive suppression ideas (early)
- Global suppression windows:
  - no more than 1 outbound task per company per 14 days unless Tier A
  - do not re-trigger the same signal type within 30 days unless severity increases by 2x

- Exclusions / denylist:
  - internal Redwood orgs + known test org patterns
  - known partners / vendors doing due diligence (need list from Partnerships)

- “Do not outreach” guardrails:
  - active support incident or P0 outage window (if detectable)
  - customer explicitly opted out of sales outreach (need to respect HubSpot field)

- Combine signals to reduce spam:
  - docs intent should usually require either (a) time-on-page threshold OR (b) repeated visits OR (c) paired with pricing page within 48h

Scoring sketch (very rough)
- Total score = scale_points + pain_points + intent_points + expansion_points
- Example weights (subject to change):
  - Spend velocity (C1): +6
  - Repeated 429s (R1): +5
  - Private/VPC intent (S5): +6
  - Security FAQ intent (S4): +4
  - Dedicated docs intent (I1): +3
  - Org expansion (O1/O2): +2
  - Size (S1): +4
- Routing threshold idea:
  - Create AE task if score >= 8, OR any Tier A single signal fires.

Stakeholder interview notes (early, paraphrased)

Stephanie (VP Sales NA) — 2025-04-30
- “I don’t want 50 tasks a day. Give me 5–10 high-confidence and we’ll actually work them.”
- Biggest wins historically: (1) 429 pain + growth and (2) compliance signals from healthcare/fintech.
- Ownership: preserve existing owner always; otherwise NA round-robin.

Markus (SE lead) — 2025-05-01
- For Dedicated: “429s + sustained volume is the cleanest ‘we can fix this’ story.”
- For Private: “Docs views are good, but only if paired with a concrete requirement (VPC, residency, audit logs).”
- SE involvement should be automatic for Private/VPC intent and for latency/SLO claims.

David (HubSpot admin) — 2025-05-02
- HubSpot rate limits are real. Avoid 10 property updates per signal; batch + idempotency.
- Keep the properties minimal for MVP: last_signal_type, last_signal_date, score, top_reasons, next_task_due.

Tyler (Marketing Ops) — 2025-05-03
- Avoid conflicting touches with lifecycle nurture.
- Suggest: when AE task created, suppress marketing “upgrade” email for 7–10 days.

Jasmine (CS lead) — 2025-05-06
- For existing paying customers, CS should be informed before outreach that changes pricing/architecture.
- “Expansion vs escalation” confusion risk: if errors are high, it might be a support issue.

Dr. Aisha (Security/Privacy) — 2025-05-08
- Keep payloads going into HubSpot privacy-minimal.
- Don’t export raw URLs with query params if they contain identifiers; prefer page IDs / categories.
- Retention: signals should have TTL; don’t keep granular browsing events indefinitely.

Open questions / TODO
1) Data availability
- Do we have reliable unique doc page view events tied to company/workspace? (Elliot + Docs instrumentation)
- Can we compute p95 latency by company without high cost / sampling issues?

2) Threshold calibration
- Need distribution cut lines from cohort analysis sheet (tokens/day, spend, 429 rate).
- How many signals/week should pilot produce per region? Target: ~25–40/week total for NA.

3) False positives
- Known example pattern: “pricing + security FAQ” from small agencies who are just comparing vendors.
- How do we distinguish “vendor research” vs “active evaluation”? Maybe require product usage above minimum.

4) Trial vs paid
- Confirm plan status source of truth + how quickly it updates.
- Do we treat startups on free credits as “trial”? (likely yes)

5) Ownership + handoff
- If an org already has a CSM owner (Dedicated/Private), who owns the task? CS-first or AE-first?

6) Messaging
- Need templates that are support-first for reliability signals (“we noticed rate limiting; want help tuning/burst?”) vs sales-first.

Next steps
- Align taxonomy + initial thresholds with Nikhil (PRD owner) + Stephanie (NA pilot) by 2025-05-09.
- Pull top 30d cohort stats and estimate weekly volume at each cut line.
- Decide Tier A triggers for MVP (suggest: R1, C1, S5, S4+size).
- Document suppression rules explicitly (to reduce spam + avoid SUP tickets).

Change log
- 2025-05-12: added time-on-page + repeated-visit guardrails for intent docs.
- 2025-05-19: raised 429 threshold proposal; added “short burst” suppression idea.
- 2025-05-27: added trial vs paid split + marketing suppression note; clarified Tier A vs Tier B.

source.title: LinkedIn Ad Creative Brief — Regulated Workloads ABM Pilot (FinServ + Healthcare)
source.body:
Slide 1 — Context / Goal
- Campaign: 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot for regulated workloads
- Verticals: Financial Services (primary) + Healthcare/PHI-adjacent (optional, pending week-2 go/no-go)
- Primary offer: “Security + Architecture Review” (15–30 min) + optional “Performance Benchmark Plan” follow-up
- Products in scope: Redwood Dedicated (reserved capacity) + Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem)
- Non-goal: drive self-serve Hosted signups (ok as secondary, but creative + CTA should bias Dedicated/Private)

Slide 2 — Target Audience (Who we want to reach)
- Primary personas (Decision + veto):
  - Security leadership: CISO, Deputy CISO, VP Security, Director Security Engineering
  - GRC / compliance: Head of GRC, Director Compliance, Security Assurance, Third-Party Risk
- Secondary personas (Champion / operator):
  - Platform engineering: VP Infrastructure, Director Platform Engineering, Principal/Staff Platform Engineer
  - ML/AI infra: Head of MLOps, ML Platform Lead, Applied ML Engineering Manager
  - Engineering leadership (in regulated product orgs): VP Engineering, CTO (mid-market)
- Procurement is a stakeholder later; not primary for LinkedIn targeting

Slide 3 — Targeting Plan (LinkedIn setup)
- Industries:
  - FinServ: Banking, Capital Markets, Insurance, Financial Services, Payments
  - Healthcare: Hospital & Health Care, Medical Practice (limited), Health, Wellness & Fitness (exclude consumer wellness), Medical Billing (as separate test)
- Company size:
  - FinServ: 1,000–10,000 and 10,000+ (separate ad sets for messaging + budget control)
  - Healthcare: 500–5,000 and 5,000+ (if we run it)
- Regions:
  - North America (US/Canada) primary
  - EMEA test: UK, Germany, Netherlands, France (only if audience size > ~30k per ad set)
- Seniority:
  - Security: Manager, Director, VP, CXO
  - Platform/ML: Senior, Manager, Director, VP
- Job titles (seed list; expect refinement after audience sizing):
  - Security: Chief Information Security Officer, CISO, VP Information Security, Director Information Security, Director Security Engineering, Head of Security, Security Engineering Manager
  - GRC: Head of Governance Risk and Compliance, Director GRC, Compliance Manager, Security Compliance Manager, Third Party Risk Manager, Vendor Risk Manager, Security Assurance
  - Platform/Infra: VP Infrastructure, Head of Platform Engineering, Director Platform Engineering, Principal Platform Engineer, Infrastructure Engineering Manager, SRE Manager
  - AI/ML platform: Head of MLOps, ML Platform Engineer, MLOps Engineer, Director Machine Learning, Applied ML Engineering Manager
- Exclusions:
  - Students, entry-level where possible
  - Agencies/marketing titles
  - “Consultant” broad filters (we can exclude some via job function/company category, but do not over-filter)

Slide 4 — Core Buyer Pains (What we are poking)
- “We want LLMs in production, but security/compliance slows everything down.”
- Auditability gaps:
  - Need strong audit logs (who did what, when, from where) + export paths
  - Evidence requests and security questionnaires create long cycles
- Data boundary requirements:
  - Keep data in VPC/on-prem; avoid sending sensitive context to shared multitenant services
  - Need encryption + key management alignment (KMS/HSM expectations)
- Operational risk:
  - Latency spikes / unpredictable throughput are unacceptable for customer-facing and analyst workflows
  - Need controlled rollouts + fallbacks to reduce incident risk
- Procurement reality:
  - Buyer needs clear packaging, support posture, and documentation readiness to get through approvals

Slide 5 — Redwood Value Props (Approved angles)
- Deployment flexibility built for regulated buyers:
  - Private deployments (VPC / on-prem options) + Dedicated capacity for isolation and predictable performance
- Security posture + operational controls:
  - Audit logging, RBAC/SSO/SAML (availability depends on deployment/product plan—see checklist), data retention controls
  - Encryption in transit and at rest; customer-managed key integrations available for Private deployments
- Reliability + serving economics:
  - Consistent performance with production-grade routing/fallback and cost/latency observability in Redwood Console
- “Reduce time-to-security-review” angle:
  - Security FAQ + evidence pack index to speed up third-party risk review (SOC 2 report sharing under NDA)

Slide 6 — Messaging Guardrails (Do / Don’t)
DO say:
- “Designed for regulated workloads” / “Supports regulated deployment patterns”
- “Private deployment options (VPC/on-prem)”
- “Audit logs, encryption, SSO/SAML (where applicable), retention controls”
- “SOC 2 report available upon request under NDA”
- “Security + architecture review available”
DON’T say:
- “HIPAA compliant” / “PCI compliant” / “FedRAMP” unless explicitly cleared for that specific claim in writing
- “Guaranteed” latency/cost numbers in ads (keep numeric claims for sales conversation / benchmark plan)
- Customer logos or implied customer endorsements (unless we have signed approval)
- “We never store your data” (too absolute; use retention controls language)

Slide 7 — Offer + CTA (What they get)
Primary CTA (preferred):
- “Book a security + architecture review”
  - Form: name, work email, company, role, deployment preference (Hosted / Dedicated / VPC / On-prem), timeline
Secondary CTA (test):
- “Get the Regulated Workloads Security FAQ”
  - Gated asset that routes to SDR follow-up with meeting ask
Notes:
- We are optimizing for meetings and SQLs, not pure content downloads
- Ensure follow-up SLA: SDR outreach within 1 business day for any form fill

Slide 8 — Creative Direction: Visual + Copy Principles
Visual principles:
- Enterprise-simple, high trust, minimal hype
- Use diagrammatic cues (VPC boundary, lock/audit trail iconography) but avoid “stock hacker” visuals
- Use Redwood brand colors, but keep contrast high and layout clean
Copy principles:
- Lead with the buyer’s blocker (security review / VPC requirement) before feature lists
- Keep copy specific: “VPC/on-prem inference” beats “secure AI platform”
- Avoid acronyms without context in headline; ok in body text if space permits

Slide 9 — Concept A (Security Review Accelerator)
Target: CISO / GRC
- Headline options:
  - “LLM inference that can pass a security review.”
  - “Private LLM inference for regulated teams.”
- Body options:
  - “Deploy in your VPC or on‑prem. Audit logs, encryption, and evidence pack workflow built for regulated approvals.”
  - “Get our Regulated Workloads Security FAQ + schedule a security and architecture review.”
- CTA:
  - “Book a review” (primary) OR “Download FAQ” (secondary test)
- Creative:
  - Simple “VPC boundary” diagram: App → Redwood Private (VPC/on‑prem) → Logs/Export
- Mandatory footnote (small text where possible):
  - “SOC 2 report available under NDA. Features vary by deployment.”

Slide 10 — Concept B (Predictable Performance for Regulated Workloads)
Target: Platform Eng / ML Platform
- Headline options:
  - “Predictable throughput for LLM workloads.”
  - “Dedicated or Private deployments for production inference.”
- Body options:
  - “Reserved capacity and controlled rollouts—plus auditability and isolation options for regulated environments.”
  - “Talk to our team about a benchmark plan and security requirements.”
- CTA:
  - “Book a review”
- Creative:
  - Performance + reliability motif: “SLO dial” + “Dedicated capacity” blocks + “Fallback policy” callout

Slide 11 — Concept C (Evidence Pack / Auditability Angle)
Target: Security + Platform (broad)
- Headline options:
  - “Audit logs aren’t optional. Neither is latency.”
  - “Production LLM inference with auditability.”
- Body options:
  - “See how regulated teams handle audit logs, retention, and key management in Private deployments.”
- CTA:
  - “Book a review”
- Creative:
  - “Audit log schema” snippet style (redacted sample fields) + “Export” arrow

Slide 12 — Landing Page Requirements (for all concepts)
- Page: Regulated Workloads landing page (single destination preferred for tracking)
- Must include:
  - Clear deployment options: Dedicated vs Private (VPC/on‑prem)
  - Short security section with links to Security FAQ / evidence pack index
  - One primary CTA with calendar or form (“Book a security + architecture review”)
  - Compliance-safe language reviewed by Security (Naomi/Aisha)
- Tracking:
  - UTM capture in HubSpot, hidden fields preserved on form submit

Slide 13 — Compliance / Security Review Checklist (pre-launch)
Content checks (PMM + Security):
- No over-claims (HIPAA/PCI/FedRAMP language removed)
- “SOC 2 available under NDA” language included where appropriate
- No absolute statements about data storage/retention; use “retention controls” / “configurable” language
- SSO/SAML/SCIM wording aligned with current packaging (confirm with Naomi Feldman / docs)
- No customer logos, screenshots, or sensitive diagrams unless approved
- “Private deployments” wording consistent: “VPC and on‑prem options” (do not imply self-serve install)
Legal/brand checks:
- Trademark use OK (no competitor logos)
- No unapproved SLA numbers
- Accessibility: adequate contrast; legible font sizes
Process:
- Draft copy review: Soojin Lee → Naomi Feldman / Dr. Aisha Rahman
- Final creative review: Camila Reyes + Security sign-off before campaign enable

Slide 14 — Measurement Plan (What success looks like)
Primary success metrics (pilot goals):
- Meetings booked rate (per form fill) and total meetings booked
- SQL creation rate within 14 days of first touch
Secondary metrics (channel health):
- CTR (target: 0.55%+ on single image; 0.40%+ on document ads)
- CPC and CPM (monitor for audience saturation)
- Landing page view → form fill conversion rate (target: 2.0%–4.5% depending on offer)
- Form fill → meeting booked conversion (target: 20%–35% with SDR SLA)
- Meeting → SQL conversion (target: 25%+ for properly targeted accounts)
Quality signals:
- % of leads in target account list
- Job function match rate (security/platform)
- Discovery outcomes: Dedicated/Private fit confirmed, security review requested, pilot scoped

Slide 15 — Attribution + Tracking Details (Ops)
- UTMs (standardize):
  - utm_source=linkedin
  - utm_medium=paid_social
  - utm_campaign=abm_regulated_workloads_2025q2
  - utm_content={concept_a|concept_b|concept_c}_{format}_{persona}
  - utm_term={jobtitle_cluster}
- HubSpot:
  - Create campaign + track influenced opportunities
  - Hidden fields on form: campaign, content, persona, vertical
- Sales handoff:
  - Auto-create task for SDR with 1 business day SLA
  - Route by region: NA → Stephanie Nguyen coverage; EMEA → Lukas Meyer coverage

Slide 16 — Budget + Flighting (initial proposal)
- Week 1–2: learn + calibrate
  - 60% spend FinServ security/GRC
  - 40% spend FinServ platform/ML platform
- Week 3–6: scale winners
  - Shift budget to best CTR + meeting rate ad set; pause any ad sets with low audience volume or poor lead quality
- Retargeting (optional if volume supports):
  - Website visitors to regulated landing page (30-day)
  - Form open/no submit (if supported)
- Note: If audience size is too small for strict titles, broaden to job functions + seniority and rely on firmographic filters

Slide 17 — A/B Tests (keep it simple)
- Test 1: CTA
  - “Book a review” vs “Download security FAQ”

title: LinkedIn Ad Creative Brief — Regulated Workloads ABM Pilot (FinServ + Healthcare)
preview:
Slide 1 — Context / Goal
- Campaign: 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot for regulated workloads
- Verticals: Financial Services (primary) + Healthcare/PHI-adjacent (optional, pending week-2 go/no-go)
- Primary offer: “Security + Architecture Review” (15–30 min) + optional “Performance Benchmark Plan” follow-up
- Products in scope: Redwood Dedicated (reserved capacity) + Redwood Private (VPC/on-prem)
- Non-goal: drive self-serve Hosted signups (ok as secondary, but creative + CTA should bias Dedicated/Private)

Slide 2 — Target Audience (Who we want to reach)
- Primary personas (Decision + veto):
  - Security leadership: CISO, Deputy CISO, VP Security, Director Security Engineering
  - GRC / compliance: Head of GRC, Director Compliance, Security Assurance, Third-Party Risk
- Secondary personas (Champion / operator):
  - Platform engineering: VP Infrastructure, Director Platform Engineering, Principal/Staff Platform Engineer
  - ML/AI infra: Head of MLOps, ML Platform Lead, Applied ML Engineering Manager
  - Engineering leadership (in regulated product orgs): VP Engineering, CTO (mid-market)
- Procurement is a stakeholder later; not primary for LinkedIn targeting

Slide 3 — Targeting Plan (LinkedIn setup)
- Industries:
  - FinServ: Banking, Capital Markets, Insurance, Financial Services, Payments
  - Healthcare: Hospital & Health Care, Medical Practice (limited), Health, Wellness & Fitness (exclude consumer wellness), Medical Billing (as separate test)
- Company size:
  - FinServ: 1,000–10,000 and 10,000+ (separate ad sets for messaging + budget control)
  - Healthcare: 500–5,000 and 5,000+ (if we run it)
- Regions:
  - North America (US/Canada) primary
  - EMEA test: UK, Germany, Netherlands, France (only if audience size > ~30k per ad set)
- Seniority:
  - Security: Manager, Director, VP, CXO
  - Platform/ML: Senior, Manager, Director, VP
- Job titles (seed list; expect refinement after audience sizing):
  - Security: Chief Information Security Officer, CISO, VP Information Security, Director Information Security, Director Security Engineering, Head of Security, Security Engineering Manager
  - GRC: Head of Governance Risk and Compliance, Director GRC, Compliance Manager, Security Compliance Manager, Third Party Risk Manager, Vendor Risk Manager, Security Assurance
  - Platform/Infra: VP Infrastructure, Head of Platform Engineering, Director Platform Engineering, Principal Platform Engineer, Infrastructure Engineering Manager, SRE Manager
  - AI/ML platform: Head of MLOps, ML Platform Engineer, MLOps Engineer, Director Machine Learning, Applied ML Engineering Manager
- Exclusions:
  - Students, entry-level where possible
  - Agencies/marketing titles
  - “Consultant” broad filters (we can exclude some via job function/company category, but do not over-filter)

Slide 4 — Core Buyer Pains (What we are poking)
- “We want LLMs in production, but security/compliance slows everything down.”
- Auditability gaps:
  - Need strong audit logs (who did what, when, from where) + export paths
  - Evidence requests and security questionnaires create long cycles
- Data boundary requirements:
  - Keep data in VPC/on-prem; avoid sending sensitive context to shared multitenant services
  - Need encryption + key management alignment (KMS/HSM expectations)
- Operational risk:
  - Latency spikes / unpredictable throughput are unacceptable for customer-facing and analyst workflows
  - Need controlled rollouts + fallbacks to reduce incident risk
- Procurement reality:
  - Buyer needs clear packaging, support posture, and documentation readiness to get through approvals

Slide 5 — Redwood Value Props (Approved angles)
- Deployment flexibility built for regulated buyers:
  - Private deployments (VPC / on-prem options) + Dedicated capacity for isolation and predictable performance
- Security posture + operational controls:
  - Audit logging, RBAC/SSO/SAML (availability depends on deployment/product plan—see checklist), data retention controls
  - Encryption in transit and at rest; customer-managed key integrations available for Private deployments
- Reliability + serving economics:
  - Consistent performance with production-grade routing/fallback and cost/latency observability in Redwood Console
- “Reduce time-to-security-review” angle:
  - Security FAQ + evidence pack index to speed up third-party risk review (SOC 2 report sharing under NDA)

Slide 6 — Messaging Guardrails (Do / Don’t)
DO say:
- “Designed for regulated workloads” / “Supports regulated deployment patterns”
- “Private deployment options (VPC/on-prem)”
- “Audit logs, encryption, SSO/SAML (where applicable), retention controls”
- “SOC 2 report available upon request under NDA”
- “Security + architecture review available”
DON’T say:
- “HIPAA compliant” / “PCI compliant” / “FedRAMP” unless explicitly cleared for that specific claim in writing
- “Guaranteed” latency/cost numbers in ads (keep numeric claims for sales conversation / benchmark plan)
- Customer logos or implied customer endorsements (unless we have signed approval)
- “We never store your data” (too absolute; use retention controls language)

Slide 7 — Offer + CTA (What they get)
Primary CTA (preferred):
- “Book a security + architecture review”
  - Form: name, work email, company, role, deployment preference (Hosted / Dedicated / VPC / On-prem), timeline
Secondary CTA (test):
- “Get the Regulated Workloads Security FAQ”
  - Gated asset that routes to SDR follow-up with meeting ask
Notes:
- We are optimizing for meetings and SQLs, not pure content downloads
- Ensure follow-up SLA: SDR outreach within 1 business day for any form fill

Slide 8 — Creative Direction: Visual + Copy Principles
Visual principles:
- Enterprise-simple, high trust, minimal hype
- Use diagrammatic cues (VPC boundary, lock/audit trail iconography) but avoid “stock hacker” visuals
- Use Redwood brand colors, but keep contrast high and layout clean
Copy principles:
- Lead with the buyer’s blocker (security review / VPC requirement) before feature lists
- Keep copy specific: “VPC/on-prem inference” beats “secure AI platform”
- Avoid acronyms without context in headline; ok in body text if space permits

Slide 9 — Concept A (Security Review Accelerator)
Target: CISO / GRC
- Headline options:
  - “LLM inference that can pass a security review.”
  - “Private LLM inference for regulated teams.”
- Body options:
  - “Deploy in your VPC or on‑prem. Audit logs, encryption, and evidence pack workflow built for regulated approvals.”
  - “Get our Regulated Workloads Security FAQ + schedule a security and architecture review.”
- CTA:
  - “Book a review” (primary) OR “Download FAQ” (secondary test)
- Creative:
  - Simple “VPC boundary” diagram: App → Redwood Private (VPC/on‑prem) → Logs/Export
- Mandatory footnote (small text where possible):
  - “SOC 2 report available under NDA. Features vary by deployment.”

Slide 10 — Concept B (Predictable Performance for Regulated Workloads)
Target: Platform Eng / ML Platform
- Headline options:
  - “Predictable throughput for LLM workloads.”
  - “Dedicated or Private deployments for production inference.”
- Body options:
  - “Reserved capacity and controlled rollouts—plus auditability and isolation options for regulated environments.”
  - “Talk to our team about a benchmark plan and security requirements.”
- CTA:
  - “Book a review”
- Creative:
  - Performance + reliability motif: “SLO dial” + “Dedicated capacity” blocks + “Fallback policy” callout

Slide 11 — Concept C (Evidence Pack / Auditability Angle)
Target: Security + Platform (broad)
- Headline options:
  - “Audit logs aren’t optional. Neither is latency.”
  - “Production LLM inference with auditability.”
- Body options:
  - “See how regulated teams handle audit logs, retention, and key management in Private deployments.”
- CTA:
  - “Book a review”
- Creative:
  - “Audit log schema” snippet style (redacted sample fields) + “Export” arrow

Slide 12 — Landing Page Requirements (for all concepts)
- Page: Regulated Workloads landing page (single destination preferred for tracking)
- Must include:
  - Clear deployment options: Dedicated vs Private (VPC/on‑prem)
  - Short security section with links to Security FAQ / evidence pack index
  - One primary CTA with calendar or form (“Book a security + architecture review”)
  - Compliance-safe language reviewed by Security (Naomi/Aisha)
- Tracking:
  - UTM capture in HubSpot, hidden fields preserved on form submit

Slide 13 — Compliance / Security Review Checklist (pre-launch)
Content checks (PMM + Security):
- No over-claims (HIPAA/PCI/FedRAMP language removed)
- “SOC 2 available under NDA” language included where appropriate
- No absolute statements about data storage/retention; use “retention controls” / “configurable” language
- SSO/SAML/SCIM wording aligned with current packaging (confirm with Naomi Feldman / docs)
- No customer logos, screenshots, or sensitive diagrams unless approved
- “Private deployments” wording consistent: “VPC and on‑prem options” (do not imply self-serve install)
Legal/brand checks:
- Trademark use OK (no competitor logos)
- No unapproved SLA numbers
- Accessibility: adequate contrast; legible font sizes
Process:
- Draft copy review: Soojin Lee → Naomi Feldman / Dr. Aisha Rahman
- Final creative review: Camila Reyes + Security sign-off before campaign enable

Slide 14 — Measurement Plan (What success looks like)
Primary success metrics (pilot goals):
- Meetings booked rate (per form fill) and total meetings booked
- SQL creation rate within 14 days of first touch
Secondary metrics (channel health):
- CTR (target: 0.55%+ on single image; 0.40%+ on document ads)
- CPC and CPM (monitor for audience saturation)
- Landing page view → form fill conversion rate (target: 2.0%–4.5% depending on offer)
- Form fill → meeting booked conversion (target: 20%–35% with SDR SLA)
- Meeting → SQL conversion (target: 25%+ for properly targeted accounts)
Quality signals:
- % of leads in target account list
- Job function match rate (security/platform)
- Discovery outcomes: Dedicated/Private fit confirmed, security review requested, pilot scoped

Slide 15 — Attribution + Tracking Details (Ops)
- UTMs (standardize):
  - utm_source=linkedin
  - utm_medium=paid_social
  - utm_campaign=abm_regulated_workloads_2025q2
  - utm_content={concept_a|concept_b|concept_c}_{format}_{persona}
  - utm_term={jobtitle_cluster}
- HubSpot:
  - Create campaign + track influenced opportunities
  - Hidden fields on form: campaign, content, persona, vertical
- Sales handoff:
  - Auto-create task for SDR with 1 business day SLA
  - Route by region: NA → Stephanie Nguyen coverage; EMEA → Lukas Meyer coverage

Slide 16 — Budget + Flighting (initial proposal)
- Week 1–2: learn + calibrate
  - 60% spend FinServ security/GRC
  - 40% spend FinServ platform/ML platform
- Week 3–6: scale winners
  - Shift budget to best CTR + meeting rate ad set; pause any ad sets with low audience volume or poor lead quality
- Retargeting (optional if volume supports):
  - Website visitors to regulated landing page (30-day)
  - Form open/no submit (if supported)
- Note: If audience size is too small for strict titles, broaden to job functions + seniority and rely on firmographic filters

Slide 17 — A/B Tests (keep it simple)
- Test 1: CTA
  - “Book a review” vs “Download security FAQ”
- Test 2: Headline framing
  - “Pass a security review” vs “Deploy in your VPC/on‑prem”
- Test 3: Creative motif
  - “VPC boundary diagram” vs “audit log snippet”
Success criteria for winner selection:
- Minimum spend threshold per variant before calling (Tyler to advise based on CPM)
- Prefer meeting rate + SQL rate over CTR if sample sizes allow

Slide 18 — Open Questions / Needs
- Confirm what language is approved for:
  - SSO/SAML/SCIM (by deployment + packaging)
  - Audit log export formats + retention phrasing
- Decide whether healthcare ad sets launch in week 3 or wait until we have finserv proof points
- Confirm landing page launch date + final CTA choice (security review call vs benchmark download)

Slide 19 — Next Steps / Owners
- Ben Carter: finalize targeting + budget split; coordinate launch timeline
- Soojin Lee: approve final ad copy (and “do not say” list)
- Naomi Feldman / Dr. Aisha Rahman: compliance/security claims review + sign-off
- Tyler Benson: UTMs, HubSpot campaign setup, reporting dashboard
- Grace O’Connor / Rohan Mehta: SDR follow-up SLA + sequences aligned to offer
- Design: produce 3 creative variants (A/B/C) + export specs for LinkedIn

Appendix — Draft Copy Snippets (for quick iteration)

- “Security review blocking your GenAI roadmap? Get the FAQ and book an architecture review.”
- “Dedicated capacity + controlled rollouts for production inference. Talk to Redwood about a benchmark plan.”
