source.title: Email sequence (FinServ) — Regulated workloads ABM pilot — v1
source.content:
Context / goal
- Audience: Financial services accounts evaluating GenAI/LLM workloads where security/compliance + predictable performance are gating factors.
- Primary CTA: 20-min “security + architecture” intro (loop in Security/SE as needed). Secondary CTA: 2-page security FAQ + evidence pack index request.
- Products to steer toward: Redwood Dedicated (reserved capacity + isolation) and Redwood Private (VPC / on-prem) depending on buyer requirements.

Compliance / claims guardrails (use approved language)
- DO say: “SOC 2 Type II available under NDA”, “encryption in transit and at rest”, “KMS integrations supported in Private deployments”, “audit logs and role-based access controls”, “data retention controls by deployment mode / contract”.
- DO NOT say: “certified for <reg>” unless explicitly verified; avoid “zero data retention” as a blanket claim; avoid implying we store/no-store prompts universally.
- If asked about specific regulatory alignment (FFIEC/GLBA/etc.): respond with “we support required controls; we’ll map to your requirements during security review” and route to Naomi Feldman / Olga Petrov.

Sequence overview (5 touches over ~12 business days)
- Touch 1 (Day 1): Problem framing + quick value prop + CTA
- Touch 2 (Day 3): Short bump + proof point (benchmark/pilot plan) + CTA
- Touch 3 (Day 6): Security-first angle (audit logs/KMS/SSO) + offer to share FAQ/evidence index
- Touch 4 (Day 9): “Dedicated vs Private” chooser + suggested next step (architecture call)
- Touch 5 (Day 12): Breakup + keep door open + resource link

Personalization tokens (use sparingly; 1–2 per email)
- {{first_name}}, {{last_name}}
- {{company}}
- {{role}} / {{team_name}} (if known)
- {{trigger}} = intent signal (e.g., “visited /regulated-workloads”, “downloaded benchmark”, “hiring for ML platform”, “AWS Marketplace activity”, “open roles: LLM engineer / security architect”)
- {{stack_hint}} = “Kubernetes”, “EKS”, “Databricks”, “Snowflake”, “Splunk”, “Okta”, “Azure AD”, “on-prem GPU”, etc.
- {{region}} = “US”, “EU”, “APAC” (only if relevant for residency)
- {{mutual_name}} = partner / intro source (only when real)

Sender / from-lines
- Primary: SDR (Rohan Mehta) or AE depending on account tier.
- When security-heavy: include “cc: Naomi Feldman (Security)” only after prospect opts into a security thread OR requests evidence.

============================================================
TOUCH 1 — Day 1 (initial)

Subject line options (A/B)
A1: {{company}} + private/VPC LLM inference (audit-ready)
A2: Your GenAI platform: lower latency without compromising auditability
B1: Question on regulated GenAI deployments at {{company}}
B2: Where teams get stuck with LLM inference in finserv

Email (keep to ~90–140 words)
Hi {{first_name}} —

Reaching out because {{trigger}} and we’re seeing a consistent pattern in finserv GenAI rollouts: teams can get a model working quickly, but production readiness stalls on auditability, access controls, and predictable performance under load.

Redwood Inference helps teams run LLM inference across hosted, dedicated, and private (VPC/on‑prem) deployments with production tooling for latency/throughput + operational controls (audit logs, RBAC/SSO, encryption, retention options).

Open to a 20‑min call next week to compare your target workloads + deployment constraints (VPC / on‑prem / dedicated) and map what Security typically asks for?

– {{sender_name}}
{{sender_title}}

PS: If it’s easier, reply with “send security FAQ” and I’ll share the sales-ready overview + evidence pack index.

Notes
- Personalize 1 line: mention workload (fraud ops, customer support, agent assist, doc summarization), or stack hint.
- If prospect is CISO/Security: lead with “audit logs + evidence pack + SOC 2 under NDA”; if Platform/ML Eng: lead with “predictable throughput + routing/fallback + cost controls”.

============================================================
TOUCH 2 — Day 3 (bump + pilot offer)

Subject line options (A/B)
A: Quick follow-up — pilot plan for a regulated LLM workload
B: Would a 2‑week benchmark help?

Email
Hi {{first_name}} — quick bump.

If you’re evaluating LLM inference for regulated workflows, we can run a lightweight 2‑week validation:
- Pick 1 workload (e.g., fraud case summarization / agent assist)
- Deploy in VPC or dedicated capacity (based on your needs)
- Measure latency/throughput + logging/audit requirements
- Deliver a short readout your Security + Platform teams can use

Worth a quick intro call to see if this fits your timeline?

– {{sender_name}}

Notes
- Avoid overcommitting on timelines. If they ask for POC credits/discounts: route to AE + Finance guardrails.

============================================================
TOUCH 3 — Day 6 (security-first: audit logs, SSO, KMS)

Subject line options (A/B)
A: Security review questions we get from banks (audit logs, KMS, SSO)
B: Can I send our regulated security FAQ?

Email
Hi {{first_name}} —

Often the fastest path to “yes/no” in finserv is a structured security review. The most common questions we get:
- Audit logging (what fields, export patterns)
- Encryption in transit/at rest + key management options (KMS/HSM integrations in Private)
- RBAC + SSO/SAML (and SCIM where applicable)
- Data retention / residency expectations by deployment mode

If helpful, I can send our regulated security FAQ and an index of the evidence pack (SOC 2 Type II available under NDA).

Want me to send that, or should we book 20 minutes with your Security + Platform leads?

– {{sender_name}}

Notes
- Only mention SOC 2 availability; do not attach reports unsolicited.
- If they ask “Do you train on our data?”: answer per approved FAQ language (typically: customer data not used to train models; details depend on deployment and agreements). Route to Security if needed.

============================================================
TOUCH 4 — Day 9 (chooser: Dedicated vs Private)

Subject line options (A/B)
A: Dedicated vs Private (VPC) — what’s closer to your requirements?
B: Sanity check: isolation + performance targets

Email
Hi {{first_name}} —

In finserv we typically see two deployment paths:
1) Dedicated capacity: reserved GPU pools for predictable throughput + isolation
2) Private deployment (VPC / on‑prem): customer-managed networking and stronger boundary controls

If you share 2–3 constraints (VPC required? on‑prem? data residency? who needs access to logs?), I can recommend the right starting point and pull in an SE for architecture details.

Do you have 20 minutes {{two_time_options}}?

– {{sender_name}}

Notes
- {{two_time_options}} example: “Tue 11:00am ET or Wed 2:30pm ET”.
- If they mention procurement early: offer “commercial + security overview” call with AE + Legal follow-up later.

============================================================
TOUCH 5 — Day 12 (breakup)

Subject line options (A/B)
A: Close the loop?
B: Should I stay in touch for Q3?

Email
Hi {{first_name}} —

I haven’t heard back, so I’ll assume this isn’t a priority right now.

If regulated GenAI inference becomes timely (VPC/on‑prem needs, audit logging, or predictable throughput for production), I’m happy to share:
- Regulated security FAQ + evidence pack index
- A sample pilot plan + benchmark checklist

Should I close this out, or circle back in {{month}}?

– {{sender_name}}

============================================================
Follow-up handling / replies (quick snippets)

1) “Send info / deck”
- Reply: “Absolutely — is your primary concern (a) security/compliance controls, (b) deployment boundary (VPC/on‑prem), or (c) performance/cost? I’ll send the most relevant 1–2 pages.”
- Attach/link: landing page + 1-pager (if approved) + security FAQ (no SOC 2 report unless NDA).

2) “We already have an LLM provider”
- Reply: “Totally — we’re often brought in when teams need more control over serving economics + deployment boundaries (Dedicated/Private) without rebuilding ops (logging, rollouts, fallback, cost dashboards). If you’re open, I can ask 2 questions and see if it’s worth a deeper dive.”
- Discovery questions: “Is VPC/on‑prem a requirement?” “Do you have SLOs for p95 latency/throughput?” “What audit/log export is required?”

3) “Are you HIPAA/GLBA/FFIEC compliant?”
- Reply: “We support controls commonly required for regulated workloads and can map our controls to your requirements during a security review. We also have a regulated security FAQ and SOC 2 Type II available under NDA.”
- Route: Naomi Feldman / Olga Petrov for formal questionnaire language.

4) “Data retention / do you store prompts?”
- Reply: “It depends on deployment mode and configured retention policies. We can walk through options (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private) and provide the exact language we use for security reviews.”

5) “Pricing?”
- Reply: “Happy to share ranges after we confirm deployment mode and required SLOs (Dedicated vs Private drives the commercial model). Can we do 15 minutes to scope?”

A/B testing notes (pilot)
- Test 1: CTA type
  - Variant A: “20‑min security + architecture call”
  - Variant B: “Reply ‘send security FAQ’”
- Test 2: Lead with
  - Variant A: Security controls (audit logs, KMS, SSO)
  - Variant B: Performance economics (predictable throughput, routing/fallback, cost visibility)
- Measure: reply rate, meeting booked rate, security FAQ request rate, and time-to-first-meeting.

Operational notes
- Sequencing rules: stop sequence when prospect replies; if they request evidence, create a Security follow-up task and tag Naomi Feldman + Daniel Carter.
- Logging: update HubSpot with {{trigger}} used and which variant (A/B) was sent.
- Time zones: default by territory owner (Stephanie Nguyen NA; Lukas Meyer/Hannah Schmitt EMEA).

Open questions / TODO
- Confirm which finserv proof points we can reference externally (even genericized).
- Finalize link destinations (regulated landing page URL, security FAQ PDF link) after Docs publishes.
- Confirm whether we want an “AWS Marketplace private offer” mention for co-sell tagged accounts (keep out of default copy unless relevant).

source.title: Vertical ABM pilot (Regulated Workloads) — week-by-week plan + owners (working doc)
source.content:
Doc purpose
- Single source of truth for the 6–8 week Vertical ABM pilot (regulated workloads). This is a working plan (will change).
- Goals: (1) validate repeatable positioning + outbound motion for security/compliance-driven buyers, (2) produce durable vertical assets, (3) generate qualified pipeline for Dedicated + Private.
- Primary vertical (default): Financial services. Secondary vertical (conditional): Healthcare/PHI-adjacent.

Pilot guardrails (please read)
- Out of scope: major new product features. Only small docs updates, packaging clarifications, and enablement work.
- Compliance accuracy is non-negotiable: no “HIPAA-certified/HIPAA-ready” language; avoid implying certifications we don’t have. Use approved claims only.
- Evidence pack sharing must follow confidentiality guidance; SOC 2 report only via approved process.

Owners (quick map)
- Exec sponsor (Marketing): Camila Reyes
- ABM pilot lead / program mgmt: Ben Carter
- Messaging/positioning approvals: Soojin Lee (with Dr. Aisha Rahman / Naomi Feldman for security claim review)
- Marketing ops + attribution: Tyler Benson
- SDR program / outbound execution: Grace O’Connor + Rohan Mehta
- Sales coverage: Stephanie Nguyen (NA), Lukas Meyer (EMEA) + AEs (Avery Johnson, Priyom Das, Hannah Schmitt, Diego Fernandez)
- SE/technical: Markus Klein (lead), Fatima Noor (regulated lead), Simran Kaur (arch/demo), Evan Scott (pilot execution), Colin O’Donnell (Private discovery)
- Security/GRC + evidence: Dr. Aisha Rahman (exec), Naomi Feldman (product alignment), Olga Petrov (questionnaires), Daniel Carter (evidence pack)
- Docs: Elliot Price (oversight), Kira Thompson (updates)
- Legal/procurement: Michael Grant + Sofia Mendes
- CS handoff for active pilots: Rachel Kim + Liam O’Rourke

Weekly cadence (standing meetings)
- Weekly ABM standup (30 min): Tue 9:30am PT — Ben (run), Camila, Soojin, Tyler, Grace, Stephanie/Lukas rep, Markus rep, Security rep (Naomi/Olga)
  - Agenda: last week outcomes, this week commitments, risks/blockers, account priority changes, security review queue.
- Pipeline review (30 min): Thu 11:00am PT — Sales leaders + Ben + Tyler + Isabelle
  - Agenda: meetings booked, SQL movement, stage velocity, next-step commitments, stuck deals.
- Security office hours (45 min): Wed 1:00pm PT — Naomi + Olga + Daniel (plus rotating SE)
  - Purpose: fast turnaround on questionnaires/evidence asks; pre-wire answers to avoid one-off messaging.
- Creative/content review (30 min): Mon 10:00am PT — Soojin + Ethan Wallace + Elliot + Security reviewer
  - Purpose: approve external-facing copy; lock “approved language” snippets.

Decision points (go/no-go)
- Go/No-Go #1 (end of Week 2): Ready to launch regulated landing page + outbound (FinServ)
  - Criteria: messaging approved, security FAQ ready, evidence pack index available, target accounts assigned to AEs/SDRs, tracking/UTMs live.
- Go/No-Go #2 (mid Week 4): Expand pilot into Healthcare
  - Criteria (minimum):
    - FinServ: ≥ 6 first meetings booked OR ≥ 3 security reviews scheduled OR ≥ 2 SQLs created
    - Security capacity: confirmed ability to respond within 3 business days on questionnaires
    - Assets: healthcare one-pager draft reviewed by Security/Legal (at least “safe to share”)
    - SDR throughput: sequences running with deliverability stable (bounce rate < 3%, spam complaints minimal)
  - Decision owner: Camila + Ben (recommendation from Soojin/Security/Sales)

Week-by-week plan (8-week version)

Week 1 — Prep kickoff + ICP + measurement foundation (May 1–May 9)
- Workstream: Targeting / accounts
  - Build v1 target account list (FinServ) + tiering (T1/T2/T3), and identify warm intros
  - Owner: Ben + Grace + Isabelle; Sales input: Stephanie/Lukas
  - Due: Fri May 9
  - Notes: include region + cloud preference where known (AWS/Azure/GCP), compliance flags (SOC2, residency), deployment preference (VPC/on-prem).
- Workstream: Messaging + claims approval
  - Finalize regulated messaging spine + “approved language” and “do not say” list
  - Owner: Soojin; Review: Naomi + Dr. Aisha Rahman
  - Due: Thu May 8
- Workstream: Security assets
  - Update regulated security FAQ and evidence pack index (what’s available + how to request)
  - Owner: Naomi + Daniel + Olga
  - Due: Fri May 9
- Workstream: Measurement
  - Define KPIs + tracking (UTMs, HubSpot properties, meeting attribution rules)
  - Owner: Tyler + Isabelle
  - Due: Fri May 9

Week 2 — Asset build + outbound readiness (May 12–May 16)
- Landing page copy/design final review (regulated workloads)
  - Owner: Ethan Wallace + Elliot; Approvals: Soojin + Security
  - Due: Wed May 14 (copy lock), Fri May 16 (publish)

  - Owner: Ethan; Review: Markus + Security + Legal (light touch)
  - Due: Fri May 16 (v1 “safe to share”)

  - Owner: Grace + Rohan; PMM support: Soojin

  - Owner: Soojin + Stephanie (field feedback)
  - Due: Fri May 16

  - Owner: Camila + Ben

  - Due: Launch Mon May 19

  - Owner: Ben + Tyler
  - Due: Tue May 20

  - Owner: Olga + Naomi + Daniel
  - Due: Wed May 21

  - Owner: Markus + Simran
  - Due: Thu May 22

  - Owner: Isabelle
  - Due: Fri May 23

  - Owner: AEs + Markus/SE bench
  - Due: all week

  - Owner: Markus + Evan + Sales owner
  - Due: Fri May 30

  - Owner: Grace/Rohan + Ethan; Review: Soojin + Security + Legal

  - Owner: Fatima Noor + Naomi
  - Due: Wed Jun 4

  - Owner: Evan + Sales owner + customer champion
  - Due: Fri Jun 6

Week 6 — Mid-pilot optimization + partner motion (Jun 9–Jun 13)

  - Owner: Soojin + Ben; Inputs: AEs + Security
  - Due: Wed Jun 11

  - Owner: Ben + Partnerships (via existing process); Sales alignment: Stephanie/Lukas
  - Due: Fri Jun 13

  - Owner: Ben + Soojin + Markus + Naomi
  - Due: Fri Jun 13

Week 7 — Conversion push + procurement readiness (Jun 16–Jun 20)

  - Owner: AEs + Michael Grant/Sofia Mendes (as needed)
  - Due: all week

  - Owner: Michael + Sofia + Naomi
  - Due: Wed Jun 18

  - Owner: Rachel Kim + Liam O’Rourke
  - Due: Fri Jun 20

  - Owner: Ben + Tyler + Soojin; Input: Sales/Security/SE/CS
  - Due: Fri Jun 27
- Asset hardening: finalize top-performing versions, archive drafts, document “approved language” set
  - Owner: Ethan + Elliot + Soojin + Security
  - Due: Fri Jun 27
- Next-step plan: scale to additional regulated segments OR deepen chosen vertical
  - Owner: Camila + Ben
  - Due: Fri Jun 27

Deliverables checklist (with owners)
- Regulated workloads landing page (Ethan + Elliot; approvals: Soojin + Security)
- FinServ one-pager v1 (Ethan; approvals: Soojin + Security)
- Healthcare one-pager v1 (conditional) (Ethan; approvals: Soojin + Security + Legal)
- Regulated security FAQ (Naomi + Olga)
- Evidence pack index + sharing process (Daniel + Naomi)
- Outbound sequences (email + LinkedIn) + variants (Grace + Rohan + Soojin)
- Webinar outline + follow-up templates (Ben + Soojin)
- Sales enablement deck + battlecard pointers (Soojin + Stephanie/Lukas)
- Metrics dashboard spec + attribution rules (Tyler + Isabelle)
- Pilot plan template + success criteria (Markus + Evan + CS)

KPIs (pilot success criteria — tracking in dashboard)
- Coverage: % of T1 accounts with known contacts in each persona lane (Security, Platform/ML, Procurement)
- Top-of-funnel: meetings booked (target: 12–20), meeting-to-SQL rate, time-to-first-meeting
- Pipeline: SQLs created (target: 6–10), POCs/pilots started (target: 2–4), stage velocity, Dedicated/Private mix
- Conversion signals: security reviews completed, evidence pack requests, procurement/legal calls scheduled
- Quality: disqualification reasons captured (no budget, wrong deployment constraints, “just experimenting”, etc.)

Running risk log (updated in standup)
1) Risk: Over-claiming compliance posture in outbound or landing page
   - Probability: Med | Impact: High
   - Owner: Soojin + Naomi
   - Mitigation: lock “approved language” doc; Security review for all external copy; add footnotes/precision (e.g., ‘supports customer compliance programs’).

2) Risk: Security questionnaire turnaround too slow (buyer stalls)
   - Probability: Med | Impact: High
   - Owner: Olga
   - Mitigation: Security office hours + intake form; standard responses library; pre-approved evidence pack index; SLA target 3 business days.

3) Risk: Confusion in-market: Dedicated vs Private positioning for regulated buyers
   - Probability: High | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Soojin + Lauren Bishop + Vivek Kulkarni
   - Mitigation: add simple decision tree + reference architectures; align Sales talk track; create 1-slide “which is right for you”.

4) Risk: Low intent signal quality → wasted SDR cycles
   - Probability: Med | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Ben + Tyler
   - Mitigation: tighten scoring rubric; focus on triggers (benchmark downloads, security page visits, partner referrals); weekly list pruning with Sales.

5) Risk: Deliverability issues (new sequences + compliance terms trigger spam filters)
   - Probability: Med | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Grace + Tyler
   - Mitigation: warm domains; test subject lines; reduce link density; include plain-text variants; monitor bounce/spam daily first week.

6) Risk: Lack of proof points/referenceable customer stories in regulated
   - Probability: High | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Camila + Ben
   - Mitigation: use ‘genericized’ proof points and architecture wins; focus on measurable pilot outcomes (latency, cost, auditability) with permission.

7) Risk: SE bandwidth (technical pilots + deep dives pile up)
   - Probability: Med | Impact: High
   - Owner: Markus
   - Mitigation: define qualification gates for pilots; reuse demo assets; rotate on-call SE for security deep dives; schedule office hours.

8) Risk: Healthcare expansion introduces legal sensitivity (PHI language) and slows momentum
   - Probability: Med | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Ben + Legal/Security
   - Mitigation: only expand after Go/No-Go #2; keep healthcare language to ‘PHI-adjacent’ and ‘customer-controlled boundaries’; Legal review on one-pager.

Open questions / parking lot
- Do we want a single ‘regulated’ landing page with vertical modules, or separate /finserv and /healthcare pages? (Elliot + Soojin)
- What is the cleanest way to describe data retention controls without implying default retention windows? (Naomi + Legal)
- For pilots: standard credit/discount guardrails and phrasing for prospects (Finance thread) (Ben)

Changelog (light)
- 2025-05-01: initial plan drafted after kickoff; added Go/No-Go gates, cadence, initial risks.
- 2025-05-16: updated owners + tightened Week 2 deliverables and tracking requirements.

- Out of scope: major new product features. Only small docs updates, packaging clarifications, and enablement work.

  - Update regulated security FAQ and evidence pack index (what’s available + how to request)
  - Owner: Naomi + Daniel + Olga
  - Due: Fri May 9
- Workstream: Measurement
  - Define KPIs + tracking (UTMs, HubSpot properties, meeting attribution rules)
  - Owner: Tyler + Isabelle
  - Due: Fri May 9

Week 2 — Asset build + outbound readiness (May 12–May 16)
- Landing page copy/design final review (regulated workloads)
  - Owner: Ethan Wallace + Elliot; Approvals: Soojin + Security
  - Due: Wed May 14 (copy lock), Fri May 16 (publish)
- FinServ one-pager v1 (Private + Dedicated options)
  - Owner: Ethan; Review: Markus + Security + Legal (light touch)
  - Due: Fri May 16 (v1 “safe to share”)
- Outbound sequences v1 (email + LinkedIn) + personalization snippets
  - Owner: Grace + Rohan; PMM support: Soojin
  - Due: Thu May 15
- Sales enablement: talk track + discovery questions + objection handling (regulated)
  - Owner: Soojin + Stephanie (field feedback)
  - Due: Fri May 16
- GO/NO-GO #1: Launch readiness (end of week)
  - Owner: Camila + Ben

Week 3 — Launch outreach + first meetings + security review process (May 19–May 23)
- Start outbound to FinServ T1 accounts (SDR + AE-sourced)
  - Owner: Grace/Rohan + AEs (Avery, Priyom, Hannah, Diego)
  - Due: Launch Mon May 19
- Activate LinkedIn tests (small budget) targeting: CISO/Security, Head of Platform, ML Eng
  - Owner: Ben + Tyler
  - Due: Tue May 20
- Create “security review fast lane” intake form + routing (who answers what)
  - Owner: Olga + Naomi + Daniel
  - Due: Wed May 21
- SE demo readiness: regulated architecture storyline (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private), with emphasis on audit logs, SSO/SAML, KMS/HSM, residency
  - Owner: Markus + Simran
  - Due: Thu May 22
- Pipeline hygiene: ensure target accounts mapped + correct lifecycle stages
  - Owner: Isabelle
  - Due: Fri May 23

Week 4 — Execution + early pilots + Go/No-Go #2 (May 26–May 30)
- Run first wave discovery + tailored demos (FinServ)
  - Owner: AEs + Markus/SE bench
  - Due: all week
- Identify 1–2 pilot candidates (benchmark + architecture validation)
  - Owner: Markus + Evan + Sales owner
  - Due: Fri May 30
- Publish audit log export example snippet (supporting technical deep dives)
  - Owner: Kira + Markus (align with GitHub examples)
  - Due: Thu May 29
- GO/NO-GO #2: Expand into Healthcare
  - Owner: Camila + Ben
  - Meeting: Thu May 29 standup slot (extend to 45 min)

Week 5 — Expand volume + verticalization (Jun 2–Jun 6)
- If approved: start Healthcare outreach to T1/T2 + finalize healthcare one-pager v1
  - Owner: Grace/Rohan + Ethan; Review: Soojin + Security + Legal
  - Due: Outreach start Mon Jun 2; one-pager by Fri Jun 6
- Host 1 technical deep dive session (audit logs + KMS + retention controls)
  - Owner: Fatima Noor + Naomi
  - Due: Wed Jun 4
- Pilot execution: success criteria doc + dataset constraints (esp. PHI-adjacent)
  - Owner: Evan + Sales owner + customer champion
  - Due: Fri Jun 6

Week 6 — Mid-pilot optimization + partner motion (Jun 9–Jun 13)
- Tune messaging based on objections encountered (packaging: Dedicated vs Private; residency; retention)
  - Owner: Soojin + Ben; Inputs: AEs + Security
  - Due: Wed Jun 11
- Partner channel: identify co-sell candidates (AWS Marketplace private offer where relevant)
  - Owner: Ben + Partnerships (via existing process); Sales alignment: Stephanie/Lukas
  - Due: Fri Jun 13
- Webinar build: finalize agenda, speakers, demo flow, compliance review checklist
  - Owner: Ben + Soojin + Markus + Naomi
  - Due: Fri Jun 13

Week 7 — Conversion push + procurement readiness (Jun 16–Jun 20)
- Focus on moving qualified accounts: security review → pilot proposal → procurement path
  - Owner: AEs + Michael Grant/Sofia Mendes (as needed)
  - Due: all week
- Create “procurement-ready” packet checklist (DPA/SLA FAQs, residency/retention language pointers, evidence request process)
  - Owner: Michael + Sofia + Naomi
  - Due: Wed Jun 18
- CS handoff readiness for any active pilots moving to implementation
  - Owner: Rachel Kim + Liam O’Rourke
  - Due: Fri Jun 20

Week 8 — Close + retro + next iteration plan (Jun 23–Jun 27)
- Post-pilot readout: outcomes, what converted, what didn’t, recommendations
  - Owner: Ben + Tyler + Soojin; Input: Sales/Security/SE/CS
  - Due: Fri Jun 27
- Asset hardening: finalize top-performing versions, archive drafts, document “approved language” set
  - Owner: Ethan + Elliot + Soojin + Security
  - Due: Fri Jun 27
- Next-step plan: scale to additional regulated segments OR deepen chosen vertical
  - Owner: Camila + Ben
  - Due: Fri Jun 27

Deliverables checklist (with owners)
- Regulated workloads landing page (Ethan + Elliot; approvals: Soojin + Security)
- FinServ one-pager v1 (Ethan; approvals: Soojin + Security)
- Healthcare one-pager v1 (conditional) (Ethan; approvals: Soojin + Security + Legal)
- Regulated security FAQ (Naomi + Olga)
- Evidence pack index + sharing process (Daniel + Naomi)
- Outbound sequences (email + LinkedIn) + variants (Grace + Rohan + Soojin)
- Webinar outline + follow-up templates (Ben + Soojin)
- Sales enablement deck + battlecard pointers (Soojin + Stephanie/Lukas)
- Metrics dashboard spec + attribution rules (Tyler + Isabelle)
- Pilot plan template + success criteria (Markus + Evan + CS)

KPIs (pilot success criteria — tracking in dashboard)
- Coverage: % of T1 accounts with known contacts in each persona lane (Security, Platform/ML, Procurement)
- Top-of-funnel: meetings booked (target: 12–20), meeting-to-SQL rate, time-to-first-meeting
- Pipeline: SQLs created (target: 6–10), POCs/pilots started (target: 2–4), stage velocity, Dedicated/Private mix
- Conversion signals: security reviews completed, evidence pack requests, procurement/legal calls scheduled
- Quality: disqualification reasons captured (no budget, wrong deployment constraints, “just experimenting”, etc.)

Running risk log (updated in standup)
1) Risk: Over-claiming compliance posture in outbound or landing page
   - Probability: Med | Impact: High
   - Owner: Soojin + Naomi
   - Mitigation: lock “approved language” doc; Security review for all external copy; add footnotes/precision (e.g., ‘supports customer compliance programs’).

2) Risk: Security questionnaire turnaround too slow (buyer stalls)
   - Probability: Med | Impact: High
   - Owner: Olga
   - Mitigation: Security office hours + intake form; standard responses library; pre-approved evidence pack index; SLA target 3 business days.

3) Risk: Confusion in-market: Dedicated vs Private positioning for regulated buyers
   - Probability: High | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Soojin + Lauren Bishop + Vivek Kulkarni
   - Mitigation: add simple decision tree + reference architectures; align Sales talk track; create 1-slide “which is right for you”.

4) Risk: Low intent signal quality → wasted SDR cycles
   - Probability: Med | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Ben + Tyler
   - Mitigation: tighten scoring rubric; focus on triggers (benchmark downloads, security page visits, partner referrals); weekly list pruning with Sales.

5) Risk: Deliverability issues (new sequences + compliance terms trigger spam filters)
   - Probability: Med | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Grace + Tyler
   - Mitigation: warm domains; test subject lines; reduce link density; include plain-text variants; monitor bounce/spam daily first week.

6) Risk: Lack of proof points/referenceable customer stories in regulated
   - Probability: High | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Camila + Ben
   - Mitigation: use ‘genericized’ proof points and architecture wins; focus on measurable pilot outcomes (latency, cost, auditability) with permission.

7) Risk: SE bandwidth (technical pilots + deep dives pile up)
   - Probability: Med | Impact: High
   - Owner: Markus
   - Mitigation: define qualification gates for pilots; reuse demo assets; rotate on-call SE for security deep dives; schedule office hours.

8) Risk: Healthcare expansion introduces legal sensitivity (PHI language) and slows momentum
   - Probability: Med | Impact: Med
   - Owner: Ben + Legal/Security
   - Mitigation: only expand after Go/No-Go #2; keep healthcare language to ‘PHI-adjacent’ and ‘customer-controlled boundaries’; Legal review on one-pager.

Open questions / parking lot
- Do we want a single ‘regulated’ landing page with vertical modules, or separate /finserv and /healthcare pages? (Elliot + Soojin)
- What is the cleanest way to describe data retention controls without implying default retention windows? (Naomi + Legal)
- For pilots: standard credit/discount guardrails and phrasing for prospects (Finance thread) (Ben)

Changelog (light)
- 2025-05-01: initial plan drafted after kickoff; added Go/No-Go gates, cadence, initial risks.
- 2025-05-16: updated owners + tightened Week 2 deliverables and tracking requirements.

source.title: Target account list (FinServ + Healthcare) — Regulated Workloads ABM pilot (v1)
source.content:
Purpose
- Working target account list for the 6–8 week ABM pilot focused on regulated workloads (FinServ + Healthcare).
- This sheet is used for: territory assignment, outreach sequencing, and weekly pipeline/coverage review.

Scoring rubric (prioritization)
- Total Score = Fit (0–5) + Intent (0–5) + Access (0–5) + Timing (0–5) + Deployment Need (0–5). Max = 25.

Definitions
- Fit: Regulated workload likelihood + scale (transaction volume, call center load, internal ops) + engineering maturity.
- Intent: Evidence of active search (website visits, benchmark downloads, webinar reg, partner co-sell signal, inbound security questions).
- Access: Known champions / reachable personas (Platform/ML Eng, Security/GRC, Proc/Legal) + warm intros.
- Timing: Stated/observed timeline for evaluation/purchase in 0–6 months.
- Deployment Need: Strong signal for Dedicated/Private (VPC, on-prem, KMS/HSM, audit logs, residency/retention).

Outreach status codes
- Not started | Touch 1 sent | Touch 2 sent | Touch 3 sent | Connected (LI) | Meeting scheduled | Discovery complete | Security review in progress | POC scoping | POC active | Procurement | Closed-lost | Nurture

Column key
- Segment: Enterprise / Mid-market
- Region: NA / EMEA
- Known stack: Cloud, current model provider(s), any inference stack, observability, security tooling
- Intent signals: Short bullets; use the same vocabulary as the intent-signal doc (e.g., “Benchmark DL”, “Security FAQ view”, “AWS co-sell”, “Pricing page returner”)
- Primary contacts: Name (if known), title(s). If unknown, list target job titles.
- Owner: AE/SDR owner for next action.

Account list (v1)
| Priority | Account | Vertical | Segment | Region | Known stack / environment | Likely use case | Deployment requirement hypothesis | Intent signals (last 30–45d) | Primary contacts (known / targets) | Owner | Outreach status | Last touch | Next action | Fit | Intent | Access | Timing | Deploy need | Total | Notes |
|---:|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---:|---|
| 1 | Northbridge Banking | FinServ (Bank) | Enterprise | NA | AWS; strict VPC controls; centralized platform team; Splunk | Internal knowledge assistant + customer support summarization | Private (VPC) strongly preferred; Dedicated possible if isolation satisfied | Discovery complete; requested SOC2 + audit log details; multiple stakeholder attendance | (Known) VP Platform Eng; (Targets) CISO/GRC lead, Head of AppSec, Procurement | Avery Johnson | POC active | 2025-05-02 | Confirm security review date; send evidence pack index + MAP | 5 | 5 | 4 | 4 | 5 | 23 | High traction; avoid over-promising “residency by default”—confirm region + retention choices in writing |
| 2 | Trident Financial Services | FinServ (Financial services) | Enterprise | NA | Hybrid (AWS + on-prem); ServiceNow; Okta | Advisor copilots + doc extraction | Private (VPC/on-prem) likely; legal heavy | Procurement/legal call scheduled; DPA/SLA questions | (Targets) CISO, Vendor risk, Platform Eng Director, Legal counsel | Priyom Das | Procurement | 2025-05-01 | Prep redline response (SLA/DPA/residency/retention) w/ Legal | 5 | 4 | 3 | 4 | 5 | 21 | Keep scope tight: pilot credits guardrails; align on Marketplace vs direct procurement path |
| 3 | Brightline Insurance | FinServ (Insurance) | Enterprise | NA | Azure; Sentinel; strict auditability; SSO/SAML | Claims summarization + agent assist | Dedicated or Private; audit logs + retention controls central | Security review requested; evidence list compiled | (Targets) Head of Security Engineering, IAM lead, Platform Eng | Diego Fernandez | Security review in progress | 2025-05-02 | Deliver audit log schema summary + retention policy statement | 5 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 5 | 20 | Security bandwidth risk; ensure Naomi/Daniel looped for evidence handling |
| 4 | Orion Payments | FinServ (Payments) | Enterprise | NA | AWS; microservices; latency-sensitive; VPC isolation interest | Fraud ops triage + support agent assist | Dedicated (predictable throughput) + VPC controls; Private optional | Demo completed; asked about routing/fallback + regional isolation | (Known) Director, ML Platform; (Targets) Security architect, Procurement | Hannah Schmitt | Discovery complete | 2025-05-02 | Propose 2-week benchmark + architecture validation plan | 4 | 4 | 3 | 4 | 4 | 19 | Lead with latency/cost + graceful degradation; keep compliance claims precise |
| 5 | Regal Wealth Management | FinServ (Wealth) | Mid-market | NA | AWS; Okta; audit logging requirements; SIEM integrations | Advisor note summarization + compliance review | Private (VPC) likely; strong IAM/audit needs | Technical deep dive on audit logs/KMS scheduled | (Targets) CISO, IAM lead, Head of Data/ML | Avery Johnson | Meeting scheduled | 2025-05-02 | Send pre-read: audit log fields + KMS/HSM integration overview | 4 | 4 | 2 | 3 | 5 | 18 | Access is thin; need champion on platform side; consider partner intro |
| 6 | Helix Health System | Healthcare (Provider) | Enterprise | NA | AWS; VPC; central IT security; data governance | Clinical summarization (PHI-adjacent) + patient support drafts | Private (VPC) preferred; strict retention/residency | POC scoping underway; multiple departments involved | (Targets) IT Security Director, App Platform lead, Compliance officer | Priyom Das | POC scoping | 2025-05-02 | Confirm PHI boundaries; align on eval dataset handling + logging | 5 | 4 | 3 | 3 | 4 | 19 | Be careful with HIPAA wording; position as “supports compliance requirements” not “certified” |
| 7 | Cedarstone Health | Healthcare (Provider network) | Enterprise | NA | On-prem constraints; limited outbound internet; TLS inspection | Clinical ops summarization + coding assist | On-prem / air-gapped interest; Private (on-prem) | Requested technical deep dive; integration questions (networking, TLS, logs) | (Targets) Infrastructure lead, Security architect, Clinical apps owner | Hannah Schmitt | Touch 2 sent | 2025-05-02 | Schedule SE-led architecture call; share Private deployment overview | 5 | 3 | 2 | 3 | 5 | 18 | High fit but likely long sales cycle; qualify budget + procurement path early |
| 8 | Aurora Medical Billing | Healthcare (Billing) | Mid-market | NA | AWS; batch workloads; cost sensitive | Document extraction + coding assistance (batch) | Dedicated (cost/throughput) vs Hosted depending on PHI policy | Opened pricing email; repeat visits to security FAQ section | (Targets) Head of Data, Security lead, Ops leader | Diego Fernandez | Touch 1 sent | 2025-05-02 | Offer cost model + batching/caching benchmark; ask about retention policy | 4 | 3 | 2 | 4 | 3 | 16 | Lead with unit economics + predictable throughput; avoid heavy “enterprise compliance pack” framing |
| 9 | Summit Telehealth | Healthcare (Telehealth) | Mid-market | NA | GCP; modern app team; vendor risk process | Patient support assistant (guardrailed) | Dedicated possible; Private if residency required | Multiple landing page visits; webinar interest | (Targets) CTO, Security/GRC manager, Product lead | Avery Johnson | Touch 1 sent | 2025-05-02 | Invite to webinar + offer security review call | 3 | 3 | 2 | 3 | 3 | 14 | Qualify data boundaries and whether PHI is in scope; push for “PHI-adjacent” framing |
| 10 | Lakeview Credit Union | FinServ (Credit union) | Mid-market | NA | Unknown; likely Microsoft/Okta standard stack | Member support + internal knowledge bot | Dedicated or Hosted; Private unlikely | Light intent: single benchmark page visit; no engagement | (Targets) IT Director, Security manager, Digital banking product | Rohan Mehta | Not started | — | Research stack + find champion; start Touch 1 w/ security CTA | 3 | 1 | 1 | 2 | 2 | 9 | Keep in long tail; only pursue if intent increases |

Expanded long-list (not yet scored in CRM; add as time allows)
- FinServ: Pinecrest Bank (NA), Harborview Capital (NA), Helios Retail Banking (EMEA), Meridian Card Services (EMEA)
- Healthcare: Greenfield Hospital Network (NA), Oakridge Diagnostics (NA), Caldera Revenue Cycle (EMEA)

Routing / assignment notes
- NA: Avery (strategic finserv), Priyom (finserv/healthcare enterprise), Diego (mid-market), SDR support (Rohan)
- EMEA: Hannah (EMEA coverage; coordinate with Lukas)

Data hygiene checklist (weekly)
- Confirm each account has: correct domain, parent/child mapping, primary persona targets, last activity date, next meeting date, and “Next step” filled.
- Standardize stage naming to match dashboard spec (Meetings booked, SQLs, POC starts, Procurement).

Open questions / next edits
- Add a “Partner motion” column once AWS co-sell targets are finalized (tag: Marketplace-ready vs direct).
- For healthcare: confirm approved phrasing for PHI/HIPAA in outbound and landing page footnotes before scaling outreach.
- Add a “Security questionnaire status” column once first 2 security reviews progress (Northbridge/Brightline).
